_total_estimate = 0u64;\n213\t\n214\t        for line in data_str.lines() {\n215\t            if line.is_empty() {\n216\t                continue;\n217\t            }\n218\t            let doc: // Extract primary key value\n223\t            let pk_value = doc\n224\t                .get(&primary_key)\n225\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n226\t                .ok_or_else(|| {\n227\t                    MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n228\t                        \"missing or invalid primary key field: {}\",\n229\t                        primary_key\n230\t                    ))\n231\t                })?;\n232\t\n233\t            // Compute shard and route\n234\t            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n235\t\n236\t            // Get target nodes for this shard (assign across all replica groups)\n237\t            let target_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = topology\n238\t                .groups()\n239\t                .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n240\t                .collect();\n241\t\n242\t            if target_nodes.is_empty() {\n243\t                return Err(MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"no nodes for shard {}\", shard_id)));\n244\t            }\n245\t\n246\t            // Add to each target's buffer\n247\t            for node in &target_nodes .push(doc.clone());\n252\t            }\n253\t\n254\t            processed += 1;\n255\t\n256\t            // Flush buffers when they reach batch size\n257\t            if processed % config.batch_size as u64 == 0 {\n258\t                Self::flush_buffers(\n259\t                    &index_uid,\n260\t                    &mut per_target_buffers,\n261\t                    &config,\n262\t import_id,\n277\t            processed,\n278\t        )\n279\t        .await?;\n280\t\n281\t        // Mark complete\n282\t        Self::update_phase(&imports, import_id, index_uid\n308\t            );\n309\t\n310\t            // Update status\n311\t            let mut imports = imports.write().await;\n312\t            if let Some(status) = imports.get_mut(import_id) -> u32 {\n    1000\n}\nfn default_parallel() -> u32 {\n    8\n}\nfn default_memory_buffer() -> u64 {\n    134_217_728 // 128 MiB\n}\nfn default_chunk_size() -> u64 {\n    268_435_456 // 256 MiB\n}\n\nimpl Default for DumpImportConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            mode: default_mode(),\n            batch_size: default_batch_size(),\n            parallel_target_writes: default_parallel(),\n            memory_buffer_bytes: default_memory_buffer(),\n            chunk_size_bytes: default_chunk_size(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Dump import phase.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum DumpImportPhase {\n    /// No import in progress.\n    Idle = 0,\n    /// Reading and parsing dump.\n    Reading = 1,\n    /// Routing documents to target nodes.\n    Routing = 2,\n    /// Applying index settings.\n    ApplyingSettings = 3,\n    /// Completed successfully.\n    Complete = 4,\n    /// Failed with error.\n    Failed = 5,\n}\n\n/// Dump import status.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DumpImportStatus {\n    /// Import ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Target index UID.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: DumpImportPhase,\n    /// Documents processed so far.\n    pub documents_processed: u64,\n    /// Total documents (estimated).\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    /// Bytes read so far.\n    pub bytes_read: u64,\n    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n    /// Error message if any.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Dump import manager.\npub struct DumpImportManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: DumpImportConfig,\n    /// Active imports (ID -> status).\n    active_imports: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n    /// Topology for routing.\n    topology: Arc<Topology>,\n}\n\nimpl DumpImportManager {\n    /// Create a new dump import manager.\n    pub fn new(config: DumpImportConfig, topology: Arc<Topology>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            active_imports: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            topology,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start a streaming dump import.\n    pub async fn start_import(\n        &self,\n        index_uid: String,\n        dump_data: Vec<u8>,\n        primary_key: String,\n        shard_count: u32,\n    ) -> Result<String> {\n        if self.config.mode != \"streaming\" {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\n                \"streaming dump import is disabled\".into(),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let import_id = format!(\"dump-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let now = millis_now();\n\n        // Create initial status\n        let status = DumpImportStatus {\n            id: import_id.clone(),\n            index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n            phase: DumpImportPhase::Reading,\n            documents_processed: 0,\n            total_documents: 0,\n            bytes_read: 0,\n            phase_started_at: now,\n            error: None,\n        };\n\n        {\n            let mut imports = self.active_imports.write().await;\n            imports.insert(import_id.clone(), status);\n        }\n\n        // Clone import_id before moving into the async block\n        let import_id_for_spawn = import_id.clone();\n\n        // Spawn background import task\n        let imports = self.active_imports.clone();\n        let topology = self.topology.clone();\n        let config = self.config.clone();\n\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            if let Err(e) = Self::run_import(\n                &import_id_for_spawn,\n                index_uid,\n                dump_data,\n                primary_key,\n                shard_count,\n                topology,\n                config,\n                imports,\n            )\n            .await\n            {\n                tracing::error!(\"Dump import {} failed: {}\", import_id_for_spawn, e);\n            }\n        });\n\n        Ok(import_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the status of an import.\n    pub async fn get_status(&self, import_id: &str) -> Option<DumpImportStatus> {\n        let imports = self.active_imports.read().await;\n        imports.get(import_id).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Run the import pipeline.\n    async fn run_import(\n        import_id: &str,\n        index_uid: String,\n        dump_data: Vec<u8>,\n        primary_key: String,\n        shard_count: u32,\n        topology: Arc<Topology>,\n        config: DumpImportConfig,\n        imports: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        // Update phase to reading\n        Self::update_phase(&imports, import_id, DumpImportPhase::Reading).await;\n\n        // Parse NDJSON and route documents\n        let data_str = std::str::from_utf8(&dump_data)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"invalid UTF-8 in dump: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Per-target buffers\n        let mut per_target_buffers: HashMap<(NodeId, u32), Vec<serde_json::Value>> =\n            HashMap::new();\n\n        let mut processed = 0u64;\n        let _total_estimate = 0u64;\n\n        for line in data_str.lines() {\n            if line.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n            let doc: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(line).map_err(|e| {\n                MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"invalid JSON in dump: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n\n            // Extract primary key value\n            let pk_value = doc\n                .get(&primary_key)\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .ok_or_else(|| {\n                    MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                        \"missing or invalid primary key field: {}\",\n                        primary_key\n                    ))\n                })?;\n\n            // Compute shard and route\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n\n            // Get target nodes for this shard (assign across all replica groups)\n            let target_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = topology\n                .groups()\n                .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n                .collect();\n\n            if target_nodes.is_empty() {\n                return Err(MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"no nodes for shard {}\", shard_id)));\n            }\n\n            // Add to each target's buffer\n            for node in &target_nodes {\n                per_target_buffers\n                    .entry((node.clone(), shard_id))\n                    .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n                    .push(doc.clone());\n            }\n\n            processed += 1;\n\n            // Flush buffers when they reach batch size\n            if processed % config.batch_size as u64 == 0 {\n                Self::flush_buffers(\n                    &index_uid,\n                    &mut per_target_buffers,\n                    &config,\n                    &imports,\n                    import_id,\n                    processed,\n                )\n                .await?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Final flush\n        Self::flush_buffers(\n            &index_uid,\n            &mut per_target_buffers,\n            &config,\n            &imports,\n            import_id,\n            processed,\n        )\n        .await?;\n\n        // Mark complete\n        Self::update_phase(&imports, import_id, DumpImportPhase::Complete).await;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Flush buffered documents to target nodes.\n    async fn flush_buffers(\n        index_uid: &str,\n        buffers: &mut HashMap<(NodeId, u32), Vec<serde_json::Value>>,\n        _config: &DumpImportConfig,\n        imports: &Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n        import_id: &str,\n        processed: u64,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        for ((node, _shard), docs) in buffers.drain() {\n            if docs.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // POST documents to the node\n            // In a real implementation, this would use the HTTP client\n            tracing::debug!(\n                \"Flushing {} documents to node {} for index {}\",\n                docs.len(),\n                node,\n                index_uid\n            );\n\n            // Update status\n            let mut imports = imports.write().await;\n            if let Some(status) = imports.get_mut(import_id) {\n                status.documents_processed = processed;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update the phase of an import.\n    async fn update_phase(\n        imports: &Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n        import_id: &str,\n        phase: DumpImportPhase,\n    ) {\n        let mut imports = imports.write().await;\n        if let Some(status) = imports.get_mut(import_id) {\n            status.phase = phase;\n            status.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\nimpl Default for DumpImportManager {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(DumpImportConfig::default(), Arc::new(Topology::new(1, 1, 1)))\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = DumpImportConfig::default();\n        assert_eq!(config.mode, \"streaming\");\n        assert_eq!(config.batch_size, 1000);\n        assert_eq!(config.parallel_target_writes, 8);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_phase_serialization() {\n        let phase = DumpImportPhase::Routing;\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&phase).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(json, \"\\\"Routing\\\"\");\n\n        let deserialized: DumpImportPhase = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(deserialized, DumpImportPhase::Routing);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_get_status_nonexistent() {\n        let manager = DumpImportManager::default();\n        let status = manager.get_status(\"nonexistent\").await;\n        assert!(status.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_import_rejects_broadcast_mode() {\n        let config = DumpImportConfig {\n            mode: \"broadcast\".into(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let topology = Arc::new(Topology::new(64, 2, 1));\n        let manager = DumpImportManager::new(config, topology);\n\n        let result = manager\n            .start_import(\"products\".into(), vec![1, 2, 3], \"id\".into(), 64)\n            .await;\n\n        assert!(result.is_err());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":393,"startLine":1,"totalLines":393}}}

end_mins: u16,\n31\t}\n32\t\n33\timpl std::fmt::Display for TimeWindow {\n34\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n35\t        write!(\n36\t            f,\n37\t            \"{:02}:{:02}-{:02}:{:02}\",\n38\t            self.start_mins / 60,\n39\t            self.start_mins % 60,\n40\t            self.end_mins / 60,\n41\t            self.end_mins % 60\n42\t        )\n43\t    }\n44\t}\n45\t\n46\timpl TimeWindow {\n47\t    /// Parse a `\"HH:MM-HH:MM\"` string (UTC).\n48\t    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {\n49\t        let (start, end) = s\n50\t            .split_once('-')\n51\t            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM, got {}\", s))?;\n52\t        Ok(TimeWindow {\n53\t            start_mins: Self::parse_hm(start)?,\n54\t            end_mins: Self::parse_hm(end)?,\n55\t        })\n56\t    }\n57\t\n58\t    fn parse_hm(hm: &str) -> Result<u16, String> {\n59\t        let (h, m) = hm\n60\t            .split_once(':')\n61\t            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM, got {}\", hm))?;\n62\t        let h: u16 = h.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid hour: {}\", h))?;\n63\t        let m: u16 = m.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid minute: {}\", m))?;\n64\t        if h >= 24 || m >= 60 {\n65\t            return Err(format!(\"time out of range: {}\", hm));\n66\t        }\n67\t        Ok(h * 60 + m)\n68\t    }\n69\t\n70\t    /// Does `utc_minutes` (minutes since midnight UTC) fall inside this window?\n71\t    pub fn contains(&self, utc_minutes: u16) -> bool {\n72\t        if self.start_mins <= self.end_mins {\n73\t            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins && utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n74\t        } else {\n75\t            // Wraps midnight, e.g. 22:00-06:00\n76\t            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins || utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n77\t        }\n78\t    }\n79\t}\n80\t\n81\t/// Resharding configuration (plan §13.1 + schedule window guard).\n82\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n83\tpub struct ReshardingConfig {\n84\t    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n85\t    pub enabled: bool,\n86\t    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_concurrency\")]\n87\t    pub backfill_concurrency: usize,\n88\t    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_batch_size\")]\n89\t    pub backfill_batch_size: usize,\n90\t    #[serde(default)]\n91\t    pub throttle_docs_per_sec: u64,\n92\t    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n93\t    pub verify_before_swap: bool,\n94\t    #[serde(default = \"default_retain_hours\")]\n95\t    pub retain_old_index_hours: u64,\n96\t    /// Allowed schedule windows in `\"HH:MM-HH:MM UTC\"` format.\n97\t    /// Empty means any time is allowed (no restriction).\n98\t    #[serde(default)]\n99\t    pub allowed_windows: Vec<String>,\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\tfn default_backfill_concurrency() -> usize {\n103\t    4\n104\t}\n105\tfn default_backfill_batch_size() -> usize {\n106\t    1000\n107\t}\n108\tfn default_true() -> bool {\n109\t    true\n110\t}\n111\tfn default_retain_hours() -> u64 {\n112\t    48\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\timpl Default for ReshardingConfig {\n116\t    fn default() -> Self {\n117\t        Self {\n118\t            enabled: true,\n119\t            backfill_concurrency: default_backfill_concurrency(),\n120\t            backfill_batch_size: default_backfill_batch_size(),\n121\t            throttle_docs_per_sec: 0,\n122\t            verify_before_swap: true,\n123\t            retain_old_index_hours: default_retain_hours(),\n124\t            allowed_windows: Vec::new(),\n125\t        }\n126\t    }\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t/// Result of the schedule window guard check.\n130\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n131\tpub enum WindowGuardResult {\n132\t    /// Current time is inside an allowed window.\n133\t    Allowed { window: String },\n134\t    /// No windows configured — always allowed.\n135\t    NoRestriction,\n136\t    /// Current time is outside all allowed windows.\n137\t    Denied {\n138\t        utc_now: String,\n139\t        allowed: Vec<String>,\n140\t    },\n141\t}\n142\t\n143\t/// Check whether resharding is allowed at the given UTC minute-of-day.\n144\t///\n145\t/// Returns `Allowed` if `utc_minute` falls inside any configured window,\n146\t/// `NoRestriction` if no windows are configured, or `Denied` otherwise.\n147\tpub fn check_window(utc_minute: u16, config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n148\t    if config.allowed_windows.is_empty() {\n149\t        return WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction;\n150\t    }\n151\t\n152\t    for raw in &config.allowed_windows {\n153\t        let window = match TimeWindow::parse(raw) {\n154\t            Ok(w) => w,\n155\t            Err(_) => continue,\n156\t        };\n157\t        if window.contains(utc_minute) {\n158\t            return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n159\t                window: raw.clone(),\n160\t            };\n161\t        }\n162\t    }\n163\t\n164\t    WindowGuardResult::Denied {\n165\t        utc_now: format!(\"{:02}:{:02} UTC\", utc_minute / 60, utc_minute % 60),\n166\t        allowed: config.allowed_windows.clone(),\n167\t    }\n168\t}\n169\t\n170\t/// Check the schedule window against the system clock.\n171\tpub fn check_window_now(config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n172\t    let utc_minute = current_utc_minute();\n173\t    check_window(utc_minute, config)\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\tfn current_utc_minute() -> u16 {\n177\t    let duration = SystemTime::now()\n178\t        .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n179\t        .unwrap_or_default();\n180\t    ((duration.as_secs() / 60) % (24 * 60)) as u16\n181\t}\n182\t\n183\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n184\t// Resharding load simulation\n185\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n186\t\n187\t/// Parameters for a single simulation run.\n188\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n189\tpub struct SimParams {\n190\t    /// Document size in bytes.\n191\t    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n192\t    /// Total corpus size in bytes.\n193\t    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n194\t    /// Incoming write rate in documents per second.\n195\t    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n196\t    /// Number of replica groups.\n197\t    pub replica_groups: u32,\n198\t    /// Replication factor (intra-group copies per shard).\n199\t    pub replication_factor: usize,\n200\t    /// Old shard count (before reshard).\n201\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n202\t    /// New shard count (after reshard).\n203\t    pub new_shards: u32,\n204\t    /// Number of nodes per replica group.\n205\t    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n206\t    /// Backfill throttle in documents per second (0 = unlimited).\n207\t    pub backfill_throttle_dps: u64,\n208\t}\n209\t\n210\t/// Results from a single simulation run.\n211\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n212\tpub struct SimResult {\n213\t    pub label: String,\n214\t    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n215\t    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n216\t    pub total_docs: u64,\n217\t    pub replica_groups: u32,\n218\t    pub replication_factor: usize,\n219\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n220\t    pub new_shards: u32,\n221\t    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n222\t    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n223\t\n224\t    /// Normal steady-state storage across entire cluster (bytes).\n225\t    pub normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n226\t    /// Peak storage during resharding (live + shadow full, bytes).\n227\t    pub peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n228\t    /// Storage amplification factor (peak / normal).\n229\t    pub storage_amplification: f64,\n230\t\n231\t    /// Normal steady-state write rate (actual node writes/sec).\n232\t    pub normal_write_rate: u64,\n233\t    /// Dual-write rate (phase 2 only, no backfill).\n234\t    pub dual_write_rate: u64,\n235\t    /// Peak write rate during backfill + dual-write.\n236\t    pub peak_write_rate: u64,\n237\t    /// Write amplification factor during dual-write only.\n238\t    pub dual_write_amplification: f64,\n239\t    /// Write amplification factor during peak (backfill + dual-write).\n240\t    pub peak_write_amplification: f64,\n241\t\n242\t    /// Backfill duration in seconds (at configured throttle).\n243\t    pub backfill_duration_secs: f64,\n244\t    /// Total bytes written during full reshard operation.\n245\t    pub total_bytes_written: u64,\n246\t\n247\t    /// Per-node peak storage (bytes).\n248\t    pub per_node_peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n249\t    /// Per-node normal storage (bytes).\n250\t    pub per_node_normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n251\t\n252\t    /// Hash distribution stats for old shards.\n253\t    pub old_shard_cv: f64,\n254\t    /// Hash distribution stats for new shards.\n255\t    pub new_shard_cv: f64,\n256\t}\n257\t\n258\t/// Run a resharding load simulation with the given parameters.\n259\t///\n260\t/// This models the six-phase resharding process from plan §13.1 using\n261\t/// the actual routing code to compute shard assignments and estimate\n262\t/// storage/write load. Document keys are synthesized for the corpus size\n263\t/// and routed through the real hash function to measure distribution.\n264\tpub fn simulate(params: &SimParams) -> SimResult {\n265\t    let total_docs = params.corpus_size_bytes / params.doc_size_bytes;\n266\t    let rf = params.replication_factor;\n267\t    let rg = params.replica_groups;\n268\t    let nodes_per_group = params.nodes_per_group;\n269\t\n270\t    // Build a synthetic topology for the simulation.\n271\t    let groups: Vec<Group> = (0..rg)\n272\t        .map(|g| {\n273\t            let mut group = Group::new(g);\n274\t            for n in 0..nodes_per_group {\n275\t                group.add_node(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{}-n{}\", g, n)));\n276\t            }\n277\t            group\n278\t        })\n279\t        .collect();\n280\t\n281\t    // Simulate document distribution across old and new shard counts.\n282\t    // Use the actual router hash to get realistic distribution.\n283\t    let mut old_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.old_shards as usize];\n284\t    let mut new_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.new_shards as usize];\n285\t\n286\t    // Track per-node storage for old and new shard assignments.\n287\t    // Each group stores the full corpus; each node in a group stores its\n288\t    // rendezvous-assigned fraction.\n289\t    let total_nodes = (rg as usize) * nodes_per_group;\n290\t    let mut node_storage_old: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n291\t    let mut node_storage_new: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n292\t\n293\t    for i in 0..total_docs {\n294\t        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n295\t        let old_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.old_shards);\n296\t        let new_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.new_shards);\n297\t\n298\t        old_shard_counts[old_shard as usize] += 1;\n299\t        new_shard_counts[new_shard as usize] += 1;\n300\t\n301\t        // For each replica group, assign shard to RF nodes.\n302\t        for (g_idx, group) in groups.iter().enumerate() {\n303\t            let old_targets = assign_shard_in_group(old_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n304\t            let new_targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n305\t\n306\t            for node_id in &old_targets {\n307\t                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n308\t                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n309\t                node_storage_old[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n310\t            }\n311\t            for node_id in &new_targets {\n312\t                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n313\t                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n314\t                node_storage_new[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n315\t            }\n316\t        }\n317\t    }\n318\t\n319\t    // Compute distribution coefficients of variation.\n320\t    let old_cv = cv(&old_shard_counts);\n321\t    let new_cv = cv(&new_shard_counts);\n322\t\n323\t    // Normal storage: corpus replicated across RG groups.\n324\t    let normal_storage_bytes = params.corpus_size_bytes * rg as u64;\n325\t    // Peak storage: live + shadow (both fully populated).\n326\t    let peak_storage_bytes = normal_storage_bytes * 2;\n327\t\n328\t    // Per-node storage (max across all nodes).\n329\t    let per_node_normal = node_storage_old.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n330\t    let per_node_peak = per_node_normal + node_storage_new.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n331\t\n332\t    // Write rates.\n333\t    // Normal: each incoming doc → RF × RG actual node writes.\n334\t    let normal_write_rate = params.write_rate_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n335\t    // Dual-write: each incoming doc → 2 × (RF × RG) writes (old + new assignment).\n336\t    let dual_write_rate = normal_write_rate * 2;\n337\t    // Backfill: reads all docs, writes each to new assignment → RF × RG writes/doc.\n338\t    // Plus ongoing dual-writes for new incoming docs.\n339\t    let backfill_write_rate = params.backfill_throttle_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n340\t    let peak_write_rate = dual_write_rate + backfill_write_rate;\n341\t\n342\t    let dual_write_amplification = 2.0;\n343\t    let peak_write_amplification = peak_write_rate as f64 / normal_write_rate as f64;\n344\t\n345\t    // Backfill duration: total docs / throttle rate.\n346\t    let backfill_duration_secs = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n347\t        total_docs as f64 / params.backfill_throttle_dps as f64\n348\t    } else {\n349\t        f64::INFINITY\n350\t    };\n351\t\n352\t    // Total bytes written during reshard:\n353\t    // 1. Dual-write ongoing for the full reshard duration.\n354\t    // 2. Backfill writes of entire corpus.\n355\t    // Approximate: backfill_duration × dual_write_rate + corpus × RF × RG.\n356\t    let total_reshard_write_bytes = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n357\t        let dual_write_bytes =\n358\t            backfill_duration_secs * dual_write_rate as f64 * params.doc_size_bytes as f64;\n359\t        let backfill_bytes = total_docs * rf as u64 * rg as u64 * params.doc_size_bytes;\n360\t        (dual_write_bytes as u64) + backfill_bytes\n361\t    } else {\n362\t        0\n363\t    };\n364\t\n365\t    let storage_amplification = peak_storage_bytes as f64 / normal_storage_bytes as f64;\n366\t\n367\t    SimResult {\n368\t        label: format!(\n369\t            \"{}KB/{}GB/RG{}/RF{}\",\n370\t            params.doc_size_bytes / 1024,\n371\t            params.corpus_size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024),\n372\t            rg,\n373\t            rf\n374\t        ),\n375\t        doc_size_bytes: params.doc_size_bytes,\n376\t        corpus_size_bytes: params.corpus_size_bytes,\n377\t        total_docs,\n378\t        replica_groups: rg,\n379\t        replication_factor: rf,\n380\t        old_shards: params.old_shards,\n381\t        new_shards: params.new_shards,\n382\t        nodes_per_group,\n383\t        write_rate_dps: params.write_rate_dps,\n384\t\n385\t        normal_storage_bytes,\n386\t        peak_storage_bytes,\n387\t        storage_amplification,\n388\t\n389\t        normal_write_rate,\n390\t        dual_write_rate,\n391\t        peak_write_rate,\n392\t        dual_write_amplification,\n393\t        peak_write_amplification,\n394\t\n395\t        backfill_duration_secs,\n396\t        total_bytes_written: total_reshard_write_bytes,\n397\t\n398\t        per_node_peak_storage_bytes: per_node_peak,\n399\t        per_node_normal_storage_bytes: per_node_normal,\n400\t\n401\t        old_shard_cv: old_cv,\n402\t        new_shard_cv: new_cv,\n403\t    }\n404\t}\n405\t\n406\t/// Coefficient of variation for a distribution.\n407\tfn cv(values: &[u64]) -> f64 {\n408\t    if values.is_empty() {\n409\t        return 0.0;\n410\t    }\n411\t    let n = values.len() as f64;\n412\t    let mean = values.iter().sum::<u64>() as f64 / n;\n413\t    if mean == 0.0 {\n414\t        return 0.0;\n415\t    }\n416\t    let variance = values\n417\t        .iter()\n418\t        .map(|v| (*v as f64 - mean).powi(2))\n419\t        .sum::<f64>()\n420\t        / n;\n421\t    variance.sqrt() / mean\n422\t}\n423\t\n424\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n425\t// Tests\n426\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n427\t\n428\t#[cfg(test)]\n429\tmod tests {\n430\t    use super::*;\n431\t\n432\t    // ---- TimeWindow parsing and containment ----\n433\t\n434\t    #[test]\n435\t    fn time_window_parse_simple() {\n436\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n437\t        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 120);\n438\t        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n439\t    }\n440\t\n441\t    #[test]\n442\t    fn time_window_parse_wrap_midnight() {\n443\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n444\t        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 1320);\n445\t        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n446\t    }\n447\t\n448\t    #[test]\n449\t    fn time_window_contains_normal() {\n450\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n451\t        assert!(w.contains(180)); // 03:00\n452\t        assert!(!w.contains(100)); // 01:40\n453\t        assert!(!w.contains(400)); // 06:40\n454\t    }\n455\t\n456\t    #[test]\n457\t    fn time_window_contains_wrap() {\n458\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n459\t        assert!(w.contains(1350)); // 22:30\n460\t        assert!(w.contains(300)); // 05:00\n461\t        assert!(!w.contains(700)); // 11:40\n462\t    }\n463\t\n464\t    #[test]\n465\t    fn time_window_boundary_start() {\n466\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n467\t        assert!(w.contains(120)); // exactly 02:00\n468\t    }\n469\t\n470\t    #[test]\n471\t    fn time_window_boundary_end_exclusive() {\n472\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n473\t        assert!(!w.contains(360)); // exactly 06:00 is excluded\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    #[test]\n477\t    fn time_window_invalid_format() {\n478\t        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"not-a-window\").is_err());\n479\t        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"25:00-06:00\").is_err());\n480\t        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"02:60-06:00\").is_err());\n481\t    }\n482\t\n483\t    // ---- Window guard ----\n484\t\n485\t    #[test]\n486\t    fn window_guard_no_restriction() {\n487\t        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n488\t        assert_eq!(check_window(0, &config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction);\n489\t    }\n490\t\n491\t    #[test]\n492\t    fn window_guard_allowed() {\n493\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n494\t            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-06:00\".into()],\n495\t            ..Default::default()\n496\t        };\n497\t        let result = check_window(180, &config); // 03:00\n498\t        assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }));\n499\t    }\n500\t\n501\t    #[test]\n502\t    fn window_guard_denied() {\n503\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n504\t            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-06:00\".into()],\n505\t            ..Default::default()\n506\t        };\n507\t        let result = check_window(720, &config); // 12:00\n508\t        assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }));\n509\t    }\n510\t\n511\t    #[test]\n512\t    fn window_guard_multiple_windows() {\n513\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n514\t            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-04:00\".into(), \"22:00-23:30\".into()],\n515\t            ..Default::default()\n516\t        };\n517\t        // In first window.\n518\t        assert!(matches!(\n519\t            check_window(150, &config),\n520\t            WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }\n521\t        ));\n522\t        // In second window.\n523\t        assert!(matches!(\n524\t            check_window(1350, &config),\n525\t            WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }\n526\t        ));\n527\t        // Outside both.\n528\t        assert!(matches!(\n529\t            check_window(720, &config),\n530\t            WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }\n531\t        ));\n532\t    }\n533\t\n534\t    // ---- Simulation ----\n535\t\n536\t    #[test]\n537\t    fn simulation_storage_always_2x() {\n538\t        // Regardless of parameters, peak storage should be exactly 2× normal.\n539\t        let params = SimParams {\n540\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n541\t            corpus_size_bytes: 10 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024, // 10 GB\n542\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n543\t            replica_groups: 2,\n544\t            replication_factor: 1,\n545\t            old_shards: 64,\n546\t            new_shards: 128,\n547\t            nodes_per_group: 3,\n548\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 10_000,\n549\t        };\n550\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n551\t        assert!(\n552\t            (result.storage_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n553\t            \"expected ~2.0, got {}\",\n554\t            result.storage_amplification\n555\t        );\n556\t    }\n557\t\n558\t    #[test]\n559\t    fn simulation_dual_write_is_2x() {\n560\t        let params = SimParams {\n561\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n562\t            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n563\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n564\t            replica_groups: 2,\n565\t            replication_factor: 1,\n566\t            old_shards: 16,\n567\t            new_shards: 32,\n568\t            nodes_per_group: 3,\n569\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 1000,\n570\t        };\n571\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n572\t        assert!(\n573\t            (result.dual_write_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n574\t            \"expected 2.0, got {}\",\n575\t            result.dual_write_amplification\n576\t        );\n577\t    }\n578\t\n579\t    #[test]\n580\t    fn simulation_low_cv_with_many_docs() {\n581\t        // With enough docs, hash distribution CV should be very low (< 5%).\n582\t        let params = SimParams {\n583\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n584\t            corpus_size_bytes: 1_000_000 * 1024, // 1M docs × 1KB\n585\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n586\t            replica_groups: 1,\n587\t            replication_factor: 1,\n588\t            old_shards: 16,\n589\t            new_shards: 64,\n590\t            nodes_per_group: 4,\n591\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 1000,\n592\t        };\n593\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n594\t        assert!(\n595\t            result.old_shard_cv < 0.05,\n596\t            \"old shard CV too high: {}\",\n597\t            result.old_shard_cv\n598\t        );\n599\t        assert!(\n600\t            result.new_shard_cv < 0.05,\n601\t            \"new shard CV too high: {}\",\n602\t            result.new_shard_cv\n603\t        );\n604\t    }\n605\t\n606\t    #[test]\n607\t    fn time_window_display_roundtrip() {\n608\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:30-06:45\").unwrap();\n609\t        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", w), \"02:30-06:45\");\n610\t    }\n611\t\n612\t    #[test]\n613\t    fn time_window_parse_missing_dash() {\n614\t        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00\").unwrap_err();\n615\t        assert!(err.contains(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM\"));\n616\t    }\n617\t\n618\t    #[test]\n619\t    fn time_window_parse_invalid_hm() {\n620\t        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"ab:00-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n621\t        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid hour\"));\n622\t    }\n623\t\n624\t    #[test]\n625\t    fn time_window_parse_invalid_minute() {\n626\t        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:ab-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n627\t        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid minute\"));\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    #[test]\n631\t    fn check_window_now_no_restriction() {\n632\t        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n633\t        assert!(matches!(check_window_now(&config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction));\n634\t    }\n635\t\n636\t    #[test]\n637\t    fn simulation_throttle_zero_gives_infinity_duration() {\n638\t        let params = SimParams {\n639\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n640\t            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n641\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n642\t            replica_groups: 1,\n643\t            replication_factor: 1,\n644\t            old_shards: 4,\n645\t            new_shards: 8,\n646\t            nodes_per_group: 2,\n647\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 0,\n648\t        };\n649\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n650\t        assert!(result.backfill_duration_secs.is_infinite());\n651\t        assert_eq!(result.total_bytes_written, 0);\n652\t    }\n653\t\n654\t    #[test]\n655\t    fn cv_empty_and_zero() {\n656\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[]), 0.0);\n657\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[0, 0, 0]), 0.0);\n658\t    }\n659\t}\n660\t\n661\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n662\t// Six-phase resharding execution (plan §13.1)\n663\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n664\t\n665\t/// Resharding phase identifiers (matching plan §13.1 steps).\n666\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n667\t#[repr(u8)]\n668\tpub enum ReshardPhase {\n669\t    /// No active resharding.\n670\t    Idle = 0,\n671\t    /// Phase 1: Shadow index created.\n672\t    ShadowCreated = 1,\n673\t    /// Phase 2: Dual-hash dual-write active.\n674\t    DualWriteActive = 2,\n675\t    /// Phase 3: Backfill in progress.\n676\t    BackfillInProgress = 3,\n677\t    /// Phase 4: Verification in progress.\n678\t    Verifying = 4,\n679\t    /// Phase 5: Alias swap completed.\n680\t    Swapped = 5,\n681\t    /// Phase 6: Cleanup in progress.\n682\t    CleaningUp = 6,\n683\t    /// Resharding completed successfully.\n684\t    Complete = 7,\n685\t    /// Resharding failed.\n686\t    Failed = 8,\n687\t}\n688\t\n689\timpl ReshardPhase {\n690\t    /// Human-readable phase name.\n691\t    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n692\t        match self {\n693\t            Self::Idle => \"Idle\",\n694\t            Self::ShadowCreated => \"Shadow Created\",\n695\t            Self::DualWriteActive => \"Dual-Write Active\",\n696\t            Self::BackfillInProgress => \"Backfill In Progress\",\n697\t            Self::Verifying => \"Verifying\",\n698\t            Self::Swapped => \"Swapped\",\n699\t            Self::CleaningUp => \"Cleaning Up\",\n700\t            Self::Complete => \"Complete\",\n701\t            Self::Failed => \"Failed\",\n702\t        }\n703\t    }\n704\t\n705\t    /// Parse from u8 (for storage).\n706\t    pub fn from_u8(v: u8) -> Option<Self> {\n707\t        match v {\n708\t            0 => Some(Self::Idle),\n709\t            1 => Some(Self::ShadowCreated),\n710\t            2 => Some(Self::DualWriteActive),\n711\t            3 => Some(Self::BackfillInProgress),\n712\t            4 => Some(Self::Verifying),\n713\t            5 => Some(Self::Swapped),\n714\t            6 => Some(Self::CleaningUp),\n715\t            7 => Some(Self::Complete),\n716\t            8 => Some(Self::Failed),\n717\t            _ => None,\n718\t        }\n719\t    }\n720\t}\n721\t\n722\t/// Active resharding operation state.\n723\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n724\tpub struct ReshardOperation {\n725\t    /// Unique operation ID.\n726\t    pub id: String,\n727\t    /// Index UID being resharded.\n728\t    pub index_uid: String,\n729\t    /// Old shard count.\n730\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n731\t    /// New shard count.\n732\t    pub target_shards: u32,\n733\t    /// Current phase.\n734\t    pub phase: ReshardPhase,\n735\t    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n736\t    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n737\t    /// Operation created at (UNIX ms).\n738\t    pub created_at: u64,\n739\t    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n740\t    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n741\t    /// Total documents to backfill (estimated at start).\n742\t    pub total_documents: u64,\n743\t    /// Last error message (if any).\n744\t    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n745\t    /// Shadow index UID.\n746\t    pub shadow_index: String,\n747\t    /// Verification results (populated after phase 4).\n748\t    pub verification_results: Option<VerificationResults>,\n749\t    /// Cleanup retention deadline (UNIX ms).\n750\t    pub cleanup_deadline: Option<u64>,\n751\t}\n752\t\n753\t/// Results from phase 4 verification.\n754\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n755\tpub struct VerificationResults {\n756\t    /// Live index PK set size.\n757\t    pub live_pk_count: u64,\n758\t    /// Shadow index PK set size.\n759\t    pub shadow_pk_count: u64,\n760\t    /// PKs only in live index.\n761\t    pub live_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n762\t    /// PKs only in shadow index.\n763\t    pub shadow_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n764\t    /// PKs with content hash mismatch.\n765\t    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n766\t    /// Whether verification passed.\n767\t    pub passed: bool,\n768\t}\n769\t\n770\timpl ReshardOperation {\n771\t    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n772\t    pub fn new(index_uid: String, old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> Self {\n773\t        let id = format!(\"reshard-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n774\t        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n775\t        let now = millis_now();\n776\t        Self {\n777\t            id,\n778\t            index_uid,\n779\t            old_shards,\n780\t            target_shards,\n781\t            phase: ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n782\t            phase_started_at: now,\n783\t            created_at: now,\n784\t            documents_backfilled: 0,\n785\t            total_documents: 0,\n786\t            last_error: None,\n787\t            shadow_index,\n788\t            verification_results: None,\n789\t            cleanup_deadline: None,\n790\t        }\n791\t    }\n792\t\n793\t    /// Transition to the next phase.\n794\t    pub fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) {\n795\t        self.phase = new_phase;\n796\t        self.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n797\t    }\n798\t\n799\t    /// Record an error and mark as failed.\n800\t    pub fn fail(&mut self, error: String) {\n801\t        self.last_error = Some(error);\n802\t        self.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n803\t    }\n804\t\n805\t    /// Update backfill progress.\n806\t    pub fn update_backfill_progress(&mut self, backfilled: u64, total: u64) {\n807\t        self.documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n808\t        self.total_documents = total;\n809\t    }\n810\t\n811\t    /// Calculate backfill progress ratio (0.0 to 1.0).\n812\t    pub fn backfill_progress(&self) -> f64 {\n813\t        if self.total_documents == 0 {\n814\t            return 0.0;\n815\t        }\n816\t        (self.documents_backfilled as f64) / (self.total_documents as f64)\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    /// Check if the operation is in a terminal state.\n820\t    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n821\t        matches!(\n822\t            self.phase,\n823\t            ReshardPhase::Complete | ReshardPhase::Failed\n824\t        )\n825\t    }\n826\t\n827\t    /// Get the shadow index name.\n828\t    pub fn shadow_index_name(&self) -> &str {\n829\t        &self.shadow_index\n830\t    }\n831\t}\n832\t\n833\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\n834\tfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n835\t    SystemTime::now()\n836\t        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n837\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n838\t        .as_millis() as u64\n839\t}\n840\t\n841\t/// In-memory registry of active resharding operations.\n842\t///\n843\t/// In production, this is persisted to the task store (SQLite/Redis).\n844\t/// This in-memory version is for single-pod development.\n845\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n846\tpub struct ReshardRegistry {\n847\t    operations: HashMap<String, ReshardOperation>,\n848\t    /// Index UID -> active operation ID (at most one per index).\n849\t    index_ops: HashMap<String, String>,\n850\t}\n851\t\n852\t/// Leader-coordinated reshard coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode B).\n853\t///\n854\t/// Acquires a per-index leader lease (scope: \"reshard:<index>\") and persists\n855\t/// phase state so that a new leader can resume from the last committed phase.\n856\tpub struct ReshardCoordinator<E> {\n857\t    /// Mode B operation leader with phase state persistence.\n858\t    leader: ModeBOpLeader<ReshardExtraState>,\n859\t    /// Phantom for the executor type.\n860\t    _phantom: std::marker::PhantomData<E>,\n861\t}\n862\t\n863\t/// Extra state for reshard operations persisted to mode_b_operations.\n864\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n865\tpub struct ReshardExtraState {\n866\t    /// Index UID being resharded.\n867\t    pub index_uid: String,\n868\t    /// Old shard count.\n869\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n870\t    /// New shard count.\n871\t    pub target_shards: u32,\n872\t    /// Shadow index UID.\n873\t    pub shadow_index: String,\n874\t    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n875\t    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n876\t    /// Total documents to backfill.\n877\t    pub total_documents: u64,\n878\t    /// Last error message.\n879\t    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n880\t}\n881\t\n882\timpl<E> ReshardCoordinator<E> {\n883\t    /// Create a new reshard coordinator.\n884\t    pub fn new(\n885\t        leader_election: Arc<crate::leader_election::LeaderElection>,\n886\t        task_store: Arc<dyn crate::task_store::TaskStore>,\n887\t        index_uid: String,\n888\t        old_shards: u32,\n889\t        target_shards: u32,\n890\t        pod_id: String,\n891\t    ) -> Self {\n892\t        let scope = format!(\"reshard:{}\", index_uid);\n893\t        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n894\t\n895\t        let extra_state = ReshardExtraState {\n896\t            index_uid,\n897\t            old_shards,\n898\t            target_shards,\n899\t            shadow_index,\n900\t            documents_backfilled: 0,\n901\t            total_documents: 0,\n902\t            last_error: None,\n903\t        };\n904\t\n905\t        let leader = ModeBOpLeader::new(\n906\t            leader_election,\n907\t            task_store,\n908\t            crate::task_store::mode_b_type::RESHARD.to_string(),\n909\t            scope,\n910\t            pod_id,\n911\t            extra_state,\n912\t        );\n913\t\n914\t        Self {\n915\t            leader,\n916\t            _phantom: std::marker::PhantomData,\n917\t        }\n918\t    }\n919\t\n920\t    /// Try to acquire leadership for this reshard operation.\n921\t    ///\n922\t    /// Returns `Ok(true)` if we are now the leader, `Ok(false)` if another\n923\t    /// pod holds the lease, or `Err` if acquisition failed.\n924\t    pub async fn try_acquire_leadership(&mut self) -> Result<bool, String> {\n925\t        self.leader.try_acquire_leadership().await\n926\t            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n927\t    }\n928\t\n929\t    /// Renew the leader lease.\n930\t    ///\n931\t    /// Returns `Ok(true)` if renewed successfully, `Ok(false)` if we lost\n932\t    /// leadership to another pod, or `Err` if renewal failed.\n933\t    pub async fn renew_leadership(&mut self) -> Result<bool, String> {\n934\t        self.leader.renew_leadership().await\n935\t            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n936\t    }\n937\t\n938\t    /// Check if we are currently the leader.\n939\t    pub fn is_leader(&self) -> bool {\n940\t        self.leader.is_leader()\n941\t    }\n942\t\n943\t    /// Get the current phase.\n944\t    pub fn phase(&self) -> &str {\n945\t        self.leader.phase()\n946\t    }\n947\t\n948\t    /// Get the extra state (mutable).\n949\t    pub fn extra_state(&mut self) -> &mut ReshardExtraState {\n950\t        self.leader.extra_state()\n951\t    }\n952\t\n953\t    /// Get the extra state (immutable).\n954\t    pub fn extra_state_ref(&self) -> &ReshardExtraState {\n955\t        self.leader.extra_state_ref()\n956\t    }\n957\t\n958\t    /// Advance to the next phase and persist state.\n959\t    ///\n960\t    /// Should be called after each phase boundary so that a new leader can\n961\t    /// resume from the last committed phase.\n962\t    pub async fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) -> Result<(), String> {\n963\t        let phase_name = new_phase.name().to_string();\n964\t        self.leader.persist_phase(phase_name).await\n965\t            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n966\t    }\n967\t\n968\t    /// Update backfill progress and persist.\n969\t    pub async fn update_backfill_progress(\n970\t        &mut self,\n971\t        backfilled: u64,\n972\t        total: u64,\n973\t    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n974\t        self.leader.extra_state().documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n975\t        self.leader.extra_state().total_documents = total;\n976\t        self.leader.persist_phase(self.leader.phase().to_string()).await\n977\t            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n978\t    }\n979\t\n980\t    /// Mark the operation as failed and step down from leadership.\n981\t    pub async fn fail(&mut self, error: String) -> Result<(), String> {\n982\t        self.leader.extra_state().last_error = Some(error.clone());\n983\t        self.leader.fail(error).await\n984\t            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n985\t    }\n986\t\n987\t    /// Mark the operation as completed and step down from leadership.\n988\t    pub async fn complete(&mut self) -> Result<(), String> {\n989\t        self.leader.complete().await\n990\t            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n991\t    }\n992\t\n993\t    /// Recover the operation state from the task store.\n994\t    ///\n995\t    /// Called by a new leader to read the persisted phase state and resume\n996\t    /// from the last committed phase boundary.\n997\t    pub async fn recover(&mut self) -> Result<Option<ReshardPhase>, String> {\n998\t        let existing = self.leader.recover().await\n999\t            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n1000\t\n1001\t        if let Some(ref op) = existing {\n1002\t            // Parse phase string back to ReshardPhase enum\n1003\t            let phase = match op.phase.as_str() {\n1004\t                \"Idle\" => ReshardPhase::Idle,\n1005\t                \"Shadow Created\" => ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n1006\t                \"Dual-Write Active\" => ReshardPhase::DualWriteActive,\n1007\t                \"Backfill In Progress\" => ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress,\n1008\t                \"Verifying\" => ReshardPhase::Verifying,\n1009\t                \"Swapped\" => ReshardPhase::Swapped,\n1010\t                \"Cleaning Up\" => ReshardPhase::CleaningUp,\n1011\t                \"Complete\" => ReshardPhase::Complete,\n1012\t                \"Failed\" => ReshardPhase::Failed,\n1013\t                _ => {\n1014\t                    warn!(\"unknown phase '{}', defaulting to Idle\", op.phase);\n1015\t                    ReshardPhase::Idle\n1016\t                }\n1017\t            };\n1018\t\n1019\t            info!(\n1020\t                index_uid = %self.leader.extra_state_ref().index_uid,\n1021\t                phase = %op.phase,\n1022\t                \"recovered reshard operation from persisted phase\"\n1023\t            );\n1024\t\n1025\t            return Ok(Some(phase));\n1026\t        }\n1027\t\n1028\t        Ok(None)\n1029\t    }\n1030\t\n1031\t    /// Delete the operation state after completion.\n1032\t    pub async fn delete(&self) -> Result<bool, String> {\n1033\t        self.leader.delete().await\n1034\t            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())\n1035\t    }\n1036\t}\n1037\t\n1038\timpl ReshardRegistry {\n1039\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n1040\t        Self::default()\n1041\t    }\n1042\t\n1043\t    /// Register a new resharding operation.\n1044\t    pub fn register(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n1045\t        if let Some(existing_id) = self.index_ops.get(&op.index_uid) {\n1046\t            return Err(format!(\n1047\t                \"Resharding already in progress for index '{}': {}\",\n1048\t                op.index_uid, existing_id\n1049\t            ));\n1050\t        }\n1051\t        self.index_ops.insert(op.index_uid.clone(), op.id.clone());\n1052\t        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n1053\t        Ok(())\n1054\t    }\n1055\t\n1056\t    /// Get an operation by ID.\n1057\t    pub fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n1058\t        self.operations.get(id)\n1059\t    }\n1060\t\n1061\t    /// Get mutable reference for updates.\n1062\t    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut ReshardOperation> {\n1063\t        self.operations.get_mut(id)\n1064\t    }\n1065\t\n1066\t    /// Get the active operation for an index (if any).\n1067\t    pub fn get_for_index(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n1068\t        self.index_ops\n1069\t            .get(index_uid)\n1070\t            .and_then(|id| self.operations.get(id))\n1071\t    }\n1072\t\n1073\t    /// Update an operation.\n1074\t    pub fn update(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n1075\t        if !self.operations.contains_key(&op.id) {\n1076\t            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", op.id));\n1077\t        }\n1078\t        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n1079\t        Ok(())\n1080\t    }\n1081\t\n1082\t    /// Complete an operation and remove from active index tracking.\n1083\t    pub fn complete(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n1084\t        let op = self.operations.get(id).ok_or_else(|| format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", id))?;\n1085\t        if !op.is_terminal() {\n1086\t            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' is not in a terminal state\", id));\n1087\t        }\n1088\t        self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n1089\t        Ok(())\n1090\t    }\n1091\t\n1092\t    /// List all operations.\n1093\t    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<&ReshardOperation> {\n1094\t        self.operations.values().collect()\n1095\t    }\n1096\t\n1097\t    /// Clean up completed operations older than the retention period.\n1098\t    pub fn prune_completed(&mut self, max_age_ms: u64) {\n1099\t        let now = millis_now();\n1100\t        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n1101\t        for (id, op) in &self.operations {\n1102\t            if op.is_terminal() && (now.saturating_sub(op.created_at) > max_age_ms) {\n1103\t                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n1104\t                self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n1105\t            }\n1106\t        }\n1107\t        for id in to_remove {\n1108\t            self.operations.remove(&id);\n1109\t        }\n1110\t    }\n1111\t}\n1112\t\n1113\t#[cfg(test)]\n1114\tmod tests_reshard_execution {\n1115\t    use super::*;\n1116\t\n1117\t    #[test]\n1118\t    fn phase_display_names() {\n1119\t        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Idle.name(), \"Idle\");\n1120\t        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress.name(), \"Backfill In Progress\");\n1121\t        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Failed.name(), \"Failed\");\n1122\t    }\n1123\t\n1124\t    #[test]\n1125\t    fn phase_roundtrip_u8() {\n1126\t        for phase in &[\n1127\t            ReshardPhase::Idle,\n1128\t            ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n1129\t            ReshardPhase::DualWriteActive,\n1130\t            ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress,\n1131\t            ReshardPhase::Verifying,\n1132\t            ReshardPhase::Swapped,\n1133\t            ReshardPhase::CleaningUp,\n1134\t            ReshardPhase::Complete,\n1135\t            ReshardPhase::Failed,\n1136\t        ] {\n1137\t            let v = *phase as u8;\n1138\t            assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::from_u8(v), Some(*phase));\n1139\t        }\n1140\t        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::from_u8(255), None);\n1141\t    }\n1142\t\n1143\t    #[test]\n1144\t    fn operation_creation() {\n1145\t        let op = }\n1153\t\n1154\t    #[test]\n1155\t    fn operation_backfill_progress() {\n1156\t        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n1157\t        op.total_documents = 1000;\n1158\t        op.documents_backfilled = 500;\n1159\t        assert!((op.backfill_progress() - 0.5).abs() < f64::EPSILON);\n1160\t    }\n1161\t\n1162\t    #[test]\n1163\t    fn operation_terminal_states() {\n1164\t        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n1165\t        assert!(!op.is_terminal());\n1166\t        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n1167\t reg.register(op1).unwrap();\n1177\t        let op2 = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 128, 256);\n1178\t        assert!(reg.register(op2).is_err());\n1179\t    }\n1180\t\n1181\t    #[test]\n1182\t    fn registry_get_for_index() {\n1183\t        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n1184\t        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n1185\t        let id = op.id.clone();\n1186\t        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n1187\t        let retrieved = reg.get_for_index(\"products\").unwrap();\n1188\t        assert_eq!(retrieved.id, id);\n1189\t    }\n1190\t\n1191\t    #[test]\n1192\t    fn registry_complete_releases_index() {\n1193\t        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n1194\t        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n1195\t        let id = op.id.clone();\n1196\t        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n1197\t        assert!(reg.get_for_index(\"products\").is_some());\n1198\t        let op = reg.get_mut(&id).unwrap();\n1199\t        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n1200\t        reg.complete(&id).unwrap();\n1201\t        assert!(reg.get_for_index(\"products\").is_none());\n1202\t    }\n1203\t\n1204\t    #[test]\n1205\t    fn registry_prune_old_completed() {\n1206\t        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n1207\t        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n1208\t        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n1209\t        op.created_at = millis_now().saturating_sub(100_000); // 100s ago\n1210\t        let id = op.id.clone();\n1211\t        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n1212\t        reg.prune_completed(50_000); // prune ops older than 50s\n1213\t        assert!(reg.get(&id).is_none());\n1214\t    }\n1215\t}\n1216\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"22339d7a-d95a-4445-a70a-e698973146ff","uuid":"406e94e8-fe16-4dd9-92c1-195c3b38ee1e","timestamp":"2026-05-23T08:30:59.889Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/reshard.rs","content":"//! Online resharding: window guard, simulation model, and six-phase execution.\n//!\n//! Implements the plan §13.1 shadow-index resharding mechanics and §15 OP#3\n//! empirical validation of the 2× transient load caveat.\n//!\n//! Leader coordination (plan §14.5 Mode B):\n//! - Acquires per-index leader lease (scope: \"reshard:<index>\")\n//! - Persists phase state to mode_b_operations table for recovery\n//! - New leaders resume from last committed phase boundary\n\nuse crate::mode_b_coordinator::{ModeBOpLeader, PhaseState};\nuse crate::router::{assign_shard_in_group, shard_for_key};\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse tracing::{info, warn, error};\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Schedule window guard\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// A UTC time window like `\"02:00-06:00\"`.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TimeWindow {\n    /// Start hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n    pub start_mins: u16,\n    /// End hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n    pub end_mins: u16,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for TimeWindow {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"{:02}:{:02}-{:02}:{:02}\",\n            self.start_mins / 60,\n            self.start_mins % 60,\n            self.end_mins / 60,\n            self.end_mins % 60\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl TimeWindow {\n    /// Parse a `\"HH:MM-HH:MM\"` string (UTC).\n    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {\n        let (start, end) = s\n            .split_once('-')\n            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM, got {}\", s))?;\n        Ok(TimeWindow {\n            start_mins: Self::parse_hm(start)?,\n            end_mins: Self::parse_hm(end)?,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn parse_hm(hm: &str) -> Result<u16, String> {\n        let (h, m) = hm\n            .split_once(':')\n            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM, got {}\", hm))?;\n        let h: u16 = h.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid hour: {}\", h))?;\n        let m: u16 = m.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid minute: {}\", m))?;\n        if h >= 24 || m >= 60 {\n            return Err(format!(\"time out of range: {}\", hm));\n        }\n        Ok(h * 60 + m)\n    }\n\n    /// Does `utc_minutes` (minutes since midnight UTC) fall inside this window?\n    pub fn contains(&self, utc_minutes: u16) -> bool {\n        if self.start_mins <= self.end_mins {\n            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins && utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n        } else {\n            // Wraps midnight, e.g. 22:00-06:00\n            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins || utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Resharding configuration (plan §13.1 + schedule window guard).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardingConfig {\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_concurrency\")]\n    pub backfill_concurrency: usize,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_batch_size\")]\n    pub backfill_batch_size: usize,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub throttle_docs_per_sec: u64,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub verify_before_swap: bool,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_retain_hours\")]\n    pub retain_old_index_hours: u64,\n    /// Allowed schedule windows in `\"HH:MM-HH:MM UTC\"` format.\n    /// Empty means any time is allowed (no restriction).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub allowed_windows: Vec<String>,\n}\n\nfn default_backfill_concurrency() -> usize {\n    4\n}\nfn default_backfill_batch_size() -> usize {\n    1000\n}\nfn default_true() -> bool {\n    true\n}\nfn default_retain_hours() -> u64 {\n    48\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReshardingConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            backfill_concurrency: default_backfill_concurrency(),\n            backfill_batch_size: default_backfill_batch_size(),\n            throttle_docs_per_sec: 0,\n            verify_before_swap: true,\n            retain_old_index_hours: default_retain_hours(),\n            allowed_windows: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of the schedule window guard check.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum WindowGuardResult {\n    /// Current time is inside an allowed window.\n    Allowed { window: String },\n    /// No windows configured — always allowed.\n    NoRestriction,\n    /// Current time is outside all allowed windows.\n    Denied {\n        utc_now: String,\n        allowed: Vec<String>,\n    },\n}\n\n/// Check whether resharding is allowed at the given UTC minute-of-day.\n///\n/// Returns `Allowed` if `utc_minute` falls inside any configured window,\n/// `NoRestriction` if no windows are configured, or `Denied` otherwise.\npub fn check_window(utc_minute: u16, config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n    if config.allowed_windows.is_empty() {\n        return WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction;\n    }\n\n    for raw in &config.allowed_windows {\n        let window = match TimeWindow::parse(raw) {\n            Ok(w) => w,\n            Err(_) => continue,\n        };\n        if window.contains(utc_minute) {\n            return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n                window: raw.clone(),\n            };\n        }\n    }\n\n    WindowGuardResult::Denied {\n        utc_now: format!(\"{:02}:{:02} UTC\", utc_minute / 60, utc_minute % 60),\n        allowed: config.allowed_windows.clone(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Check the schedule window against the system clock.\npub fn check_window_now(config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n    let utc_minute = current_utc_minute();\n    check_window(utc_minute, config)\n}\n\nfn current_utc_minute() -> u16 {\n    let duration = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    ((duration.as_secs() / 60) % (24 * 60)) as u16\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Resharding load simulation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Parameters for a single simulation run.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SimParams {\n    /// Document size in bytes.\n    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n    /// Total corpus size in bytes.\n    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n    /// Incoming write rate in documents per second.\n    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n    /// Number of replica groups.\n    pub replica_groups: u32,\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group copies per shard).\n    pub replication_factor: usize,\n    /// Old shard count (before reshard).\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    /// New shard count (after reshard).\n    pub new_shards: u32,\n    /// Number of nodes per replica group.\n    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n    /// Backfill throttle in documents per second (0 = unlimited).\n    pub backfill_throttle_dps: u64,\n}\n\n/// Results from a single simulation run.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SimResult {\n    pub label: String,\n    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub replica_groups: u32,\n    pub replication_factor: usize,\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    pub new_shards: u32,\n    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n\n    /// Normal steady-state storage across entire cluster (bytes).\n    pub normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Peak storage during resharding (live + shadow full, bytes).\n    pub peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Storage amplification factor (peak / normal).\n    pub storage_amplification: f64,\n\n    /// Normal steady-state write rate (actual node writes/sec).\n    pub normal_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Dual-write rate (phase 2 only, no backfill).\n    pub dual_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Peak write rate during backfill + dual-write.\n    pub peak_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Write amplification factor during dual-write only.\n    pub dual_write_amplification: f64,\n    /// Write amplification factor during peak (backfill + dual-write).\n    pub peak_write_amplification: f64,\n\n    /// Backfill duration in seconds (at configured throttle).\n    pub backfill_duration_secs: f64,\n    /// Total bytes written during full reshard operation.\n    pub total_bytes_written: u64,\n\n    /// Per-node peak storage (bytes).\n    pub per_node_peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Per-node normal storage (bytes).\n    pub per_node_normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n\n    /// Hash distribution stats for old shards.\n    pub old_shard_cv: f64,\n    /// Hash distribution stats for new shards.\n    pub new_shard_cv: f64,\n}\n\n/// Run a resharding load simulation with the given parameters.\n///\n/// This models the six-phase resharding process from plan §13.1 using\n/// the actual routing code to compute shard assignments and estimate\n/// storage/write load. Document keys are synthesized for the corpus size\n/// and routed through the real hash function to measure distribution.\npub fn simulate(params: &SimParams) -> SimResult {\n    let total_docs = params.corpus_size_bytes / params.doc_size_bytes;\n    let rf = params.replication_factor;\n    let rg = params.replica_groups;\n    let nodes_per_group = params.nodes_per_group;\n\n    // Build a synthetic topology for the simulation.\n    let groups: Vec<Group> = (0..rg)\n        .map(|g| {\n            let mut group = Group::new(g);\n            for n in 0..nodes_per_group {\n                group.add_node(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{}-n{}\", g, n)));\n            }\n            group\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Simulate document distribution across old and new shard counts.\n    // Use the actual router hash to get realistic distribution.\n    let mut old_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.old_shards as usize];\n    let mut new_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.new_shards as usize];\n\n    // Track per-node storage for old and new shard assignments.\n    // Each group stores the full corpus; each node in a group stores its\n    // rendezvous-assigned fraction.\n    let total_nodes = (rg as usize) * nodes_per_group;\n    let mut node_storage_old: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n    let mut node_storage_new: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n\n    for i in 0..total_docs {\n        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n        let old_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.old_shards);\n        let new_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.new_shards);\n\n        old_shard_counts[old_shard as usize] += 1;\n        new_shard_counts[new_shard as usize] += 1;\n\n        // For each replica group, assign shard to RF nodes.\n        for (g_idx, group) in groups.iter().enumerate() {\n            let old_targets = assign_shard_in_group(old_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n            let new_targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n\n            for node_id in &old_targets {\n                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n                node_storage_old[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n            }\n            for node_id in &new_targets {\n                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n                node_storage_new[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Compute distribution coefficients of variation.\n    let old_cv = cv(&old_shard_counts);\n    let new_cv = cv(&new_shard_counts);\n\n    // Normal storage: corpus replicated across RG groups.\n    let normal_storage_bytes = params.corpus_size_bytes * rg as u64;\n    // Peak storage: live + shadow (both fully populated).\n    let peak_storage_bytes = normal_storage_bytes * 2;\n\n    // Per-node storage (max across all nodes).\n    let per_node_normal = node_storage_old.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n    let per_node_peak = per_node_normal + node_storage_new.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Write rates.\n    // Normal: each incoming doc → RF × RG actual node writes.\n    let normal_write_rate = params.write_rate_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n    // Dual-write: each incoming doc → 2 × (RF × RG) writes (old + new assignment).\n    let dual_write_rate = normal_write_rate * 2;\n    // Backfill: reads all docs, writes each to new assignment → RF × RG writes/doc.\n    // Plus ongoing dual-writes for new incoming docs.\n    let backfill_write_rate = params.backfill_throttle_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n    let peak_write_rate = dual_write_rate + backfill_write_rate;\n\n    let dual_write_amplification = 2.0;\n    let peak_write_amplification = peak_write_rate as f64 / normal_write_rate as f64;\n\n    // Backfill duration: total docs / throttle rate.\n    let backfill_duration_secs = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n        total_docs as f64 / params.backfill_throttle_dps as f64\n    } else {\n        f64::INFINITY\n    };\n\n    // Total bytes written during reshard:\n    // 1. Dual-write ongoing for the full reshard duration.\n    // 2. Backfill writes of entire corpus.\n    // Approximate: backfill_duration × dual_write_rate + corpus × RF × RG.\n    let total_reshard_write_bytes = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n        let dual_write_bytes =\n            backfill_duration_secs * dual_write_rate as f64 * params.doc_size_bytes as f64;\n        let backfill_bytes = total_docs * rf as u64 * rg as u64 * params.doc_size_bytes;\n        (dual_write_bytes as u64) + backfill_bytes\n    } else {\n        0\n    };\n\n    let storage_amplification = peak_storage_bytes as f64 / normal_storage_bytes as f64;\n\n    SimResult {\n        label: format!(\n            \"{}KB/{}GB/RG{}/RF{}\",\n            params.doc_size_bytes / 1024,\n            params.corpus_size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024),\n            rg,\n            rf\n        ),\n        doc_size_bytes: params.doc_size_bytes,\n        corpus_size_bytes: params.corpus_size_bytes,\n        total_docs,\n        replica_groups: rg,\n        replication_factor: rf,\n        old_shards: params.old_shards,\n        new_shards: params.new_shards,\n        nodes_per_group,\n        write_rate_dps: params.write_rate_dps,\n\n        normal_storage_bytes,\n        peak_storage_bytes,\n        storage_amplification,\n\n        normal_write_rate,\n        dual_write_rate,\n        peak_write_rate,\n        dual_write_amplification,\n        peak_write_amplification,\n\n        backfill_duration_secs,\n        total_bytes_written: total_reshard_write_bytes,\n\n        per_node_peak_storage_bytes: per_node_peak,\n        per_node_normal_storage_bytes: per_node_normal,\n\n        old_shard_cv: old_cv,\n        new_shard_cv: new_cv,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Coefficient of variation for a distribution.\nfn cv(values: &[u64]) -> f64 {\n    if values.is_empty() {\n        return 0.0;\n    }\n    let n = values.len() as f64;\n    let mean = values.iter().sum::<u64>() as f64 / n;\n    if mean == 0.0 {\n        return 0.0;\n    }\n    let variance = values\n        .iter()\n        .map(|v| (*v as f64 - mean).powi(2))\n        .sum::<f64>()\n        / n;\n    variance.sqrt() / mean\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    // ---- TimeWindow parsing and containment ----\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_simple() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 120);\n        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_wrap_midnight() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 1320);\n        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_contains_normal() {\n        let w
