   expires_at: now_ms() + 3600000,\n                    user_agent: None,\n                    source_ip: None,\n                })\n                .expect(\"Insert minimal session should succeed\");\n\n            let session = store\n                .get_admin_session(\"sess-minimal\")\n                .expect(\"Get should succeed\")\n                .expect(\"Session should exist\");\n            assert!(session.user_agent.is_none());\n            assert!(session.source_ip.is_none());\n\n            // Redis handles expiration automatically - delete_expired returns 0\n            let deleted = store\n                .delete_expired_admin_sessions(now_ms())\n                .expect(\"Delete expired should succeed\");\n            assert_eq!(deleted, 0);\n        }\n\n        // --- Comprehensive trait behavior test ---\n\n        #[tokio::test]\n        async fn test_redis_taskstore_trait_completeness() {\n            // This test ensures all TaskStore trait methods are callable\n            // and behave consistently with the SQLite implementation.\n            let (store, _url) = setup_redis_store().await;\n            store.migrate().expect(\"Migration should succeed\");\n\n            // Test tasks\n            let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n            node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 123u64);\n            store\n                .insert_task(&NewTask {\n                    miroir_id: \"task-trait-test\".to_string(),\n                    created_at: now_ms(),\n                    status: \"queued\".to_string(),\n                    node_tasks: node_tasks.clone(),\n                    error: None,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    index_uid: None,\n                    task_type: None,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                })\n                .expect(\"insert_task should work\");\n\n            let task = store\n                .get_task(\"task-trait-test\")\n                .expect(\"get_task should work\")\n                .expect(\"task should exist\");\n            assert_eq!(task.node_tasks, node_tasks);\n\n            // Test update operations\n            assert!(store\n                .update_task_status(\"task-trait-test\", \"running\")\n                .expect(\"update_task_status should work\"));\n            assert!(store\n                .update_node_task(\"task-trait-test\", \"node-2\", 456)\n                .expect(\"update_node_task should work\"));\n            assert!(store\n                .set_task_error(\"task-trait-test\", \"test error\")\n                .expect(\"set_task_error should work\"));\n\n            // Test list and filter\n            let tasks = store\n                .list_tasks(&TaskFilter {\n                    status: Some(\"running\".to_string()),\n                    index_uid: None,\n                    task_type: None,\n                    limit: Some(10),\n                    offset: None,\n                })\n                .expect(\"list_tasks should work\");\n            assert_eq!(tasks.len(), 1);\n\n            // Test count\n            let count = store.task_count().expect(\"task_count should work\");\n            assert_eq!(count, 1);\n\n            // Test prune\n            let pruned = store\n                .prune_tasks(now_ms() - 1000, 100)\n                .expect(\"prune_tasks should work\");\n            assert_eq!(pruned, 0); // our task is recent\n\n            // Test leader lease\n            let scope = \"trait-test-scope\";\n            assert!(store\n                .try_acquire_leader_lease(scope, \"pod-1\", now_ms() + 10000, now_ms())\n                .expect(\"try_acquire_leader_lease should work\"));\n            assert!(store\n                .renew_leader_lease(scope, \"pod-1\", now_ms() + 20000)\n                .expect(\"renew_leader_lease should work\"));\n\n            let lease = store\n                .get_leader_lease(scope)\n                .expect(\"get_leader_lease should work\")\n                .expect(\"lease should exist\");\n            assert_eq!(lease.holder, \"pod-1\");\n\n            // Test job\n            store\n                .insert_job(&NewJob {\n                    id: \"job-trait-test\".to_string(),\n                    type_: \"test\".to_string(),\n                    params: \"{}\".to_string(),\n                    state: \"queued\".to_string(),\n                    progress: \"{}\".to_string(),\n Mode C work-queued chunked jobs coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode C).\\n//!\\n//! Any pod can claim a queued job via compare-and-swap. Jobs have claim TTL\\n//! with heartbeats; expired claims are released for reclamation.\\n//!\\n//! Large jobs are split into chunks on input boundaries by the first pod\\n//! that picks them up. Each chunk is an independent job with a parent reference.\\n//!\\n//! Applied to:\\n//! - §13.9 streaming dump import — chunks on NDJSON line boundaries\\n//! - §13.1 reshard backfill — partitions by shard-id range\\n\\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\\nuse crate::task_store::{JobRow, NewJob, TaskStore};\\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\nuse std::sync::Arc;\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\\n\\n/// Job states (plan §14.5 Mode C).\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n#[repr(u8)]\\npub enum JobState {\\n    /// Job is queued and waiting to be claimed.\\n    Queued = 0,\\n    /// Job is claimed and in progress.\\n    InProgress = 1,\\n    /// Job completed successfully.\\n    Completed = 2,\\n    /// Job failed.\\n    Failed = 3,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JobState {\\n    /// Parse from string.\\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\\n        match s {\\n            \\\"queued\\\" => Some(Self::Queued),\\n            \\\"in_progress\\\" => Some(Self::InProgress),\\n            \\\"completed\\\" => Some(Self::Completed),\\n            \\\"failed\\\" => Some(Self::Failed),\\n            _ => None,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Convert to string.\\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\\n        match self {\\n            Self::Queued => \\\"queued\\\",\\n            Self::InProgress => \\\"in_progress\\\",\\n            Self::Completed => \\\"completed\\\",\\n            Self::Failed => \\\"failed\\\",\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Job types supported by Mode C coordinator.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\npub enum JobType {\\n    /// Streaming dump import (plan §13.9).\\n    DumpImport = 0,\\n    /// Reshard backfill (plan §13.1).\\n    ReshardBackfill = 1,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JobType {\\n    /// Parse from string.\\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\\n        match s {\\n            \\\"dump_import\\\" => Some(Self::DumpImport),\\n            \\\"reshard_backfill\\\" => Some(Self::ReshardBackfill),\\n            _ => None,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Convert to string.\\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\\n        match self {\\n            Self::DumpImport => \\\"dump_import\\\",\\n            Self::ReshardBackfill => \\\"reshard_backfill\\\",\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Job progress tracking.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\npub struct JobProgress {\\n    /// Bytes processed so far (for dump import).\\n    pub bytes_processed: u64,\\n    /// Documents routed so far (for dump import).\\n    pub docs_routed: u64,\\n    /// Last cursor position for idempotent resume.\\n    pub last_cursor: String,\\n    /// Any error message.\\n    pub error: Option<String>,\\n}\\n\\nimpl Default for JobProgress {\\n    fn default() -> Self {\\n        Self {\\n            bytes_processed: 0,\\n            docs_routed: 0,\\n            last_cursor: String::new(),\\n            error: None,\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Chunk specification for a job.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\npub struct JobChunk {\\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\\n    pub index: u32,\\n    /// Total number of chunks.\\n    pub total: u32,\\n    /// Starting position (cursor or byte offset).\\n    pub start: String,\\n    /// Ending position (cursor or byte offset).\\n    pub end: String,\\n    /// Estimated size in bytes.\\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\\n}\\n\\n/// Job parameters.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\npub struct JobParams {\\n    /// Index UID being operated on.\\n    pub index_uid: String,\\n    /// Primary key field (for dump import).\\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\\n    /// Shard count (for dump import routing).\\n    pub shard_count: Option<u32>,\\n    /// Old shard count (for reshard backfill).\\n    pub old_shards: Option<u32>,\\n    /// Target shard count (for reshard backfill).\\n    pub target_shards: Option<u32>,\\n    /// Shadow index UID (for reshard backfill).\\n    pub shadow_index: Option<String>,\\n    /// Chunk specification (if this is a chunk).\\n    pub chunk: Option<JobChunk>,\\n    /// Source data location (for dump import).\\n    pub source_url: Option<String>,\\n    /// Source data size (for dump import).\\n    pub source_size_bytes: Option<u64>,\\n}\\n\\n/// Mode C job coordinator.\\npub struct ModeCCoordinator {\\n    /// Task store for job persistence.\\n    task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\\n    /// Pod ID for claiming jobs.\\n    pod_id: String,\\n    /// Claim TTL in milliseconds (default 30s).\\n    claim_ttl_ms: i64,\\n    /// Heartbeat interval in milliseconds (default 10s).\\n    heartbeat_interval_ms: i64,\\n    /// Default chunk size in bytes (default 256 MiB).\\n    default_chunk_size_bytes: u64,\\n}\\n\\nimpl ModeCCoordinator {\\n    /// Create a new Mode C coordinator.\\n    pub fn new(\\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\\n        pod_id: String,\\n    ) -> Self {\\n        Self {\\n            task_store,\\n            pod_id,\\n            claim_ttl_ms: 30_000,  // 30 seconds\\n            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10_000,  // 10 seconds\\n            default_chunk_size_bytes: 268_435_456,  // 256 MiB\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Set the claim TTL.\\n    pub fn with_claim_ttl_ms(mut self, ttl_ms: i64) -> Self {\\n        self.claim_ttl_ms = ttl_ms;\\n        self\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Set the heartbeat interval.\\n    pub fn with_heartbeat_interval_ms(mut self, interval_ms: i64) -> Self {\\n        self.heartbeat_interval_ms = interval_ms;\\n        self\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Set the default chunk size.\\n    pub fn with_chunk_size_bytes(mut self, size_bytes: u64) -> Self {\\n        self.default_chunk_size_bytes = size_bytes;\\n        self\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Enqueue a new job.\\n    pub fn enqueue_job(\\n        &self,\\n        type_: JobType,\\n        params: JobParams,\\n    ) -> Result<String> {\\n        let job_id = format!(\\\"{}-{}\\\", type_.as_str(), uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\\n        let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&params)\\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\\\"failed to serialize params: {}\\\", e)))?;\\n        let progress = JobProgress::default();\\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\\\"failed to serialize progress: {}\\\", e)))?;\\n\\n        let new_job = NewJob {\\n            id: job_id.clone(),\\n            type_: type_.as_str().to_string(),\\n            params: params_json,\\n            state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\\n            progress: progress_json,\\n            parent_job_id: None,\\n            chunk_index: None,\\n            total_chunks: None,\\n        };\\n\\n        self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\\n\\n        debug!(\\n            job_id = %job_id,\\n            job_type = %type_.as_str(),\\n            \\\"enqueued new Mode C job\\\"\\n        );\\n\\n        Ok(job_id)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Try to claim a queued job.\\n    ///\\n    /// Returns the claimed job if successful, or None if no jobs are available.\\n    pub fn claim_job(&self) -> Result<Option<ClaimedJob>> {\\n        // List queued jobs\\n        let queued_jobs = self.task_store.list_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())?;\\n\\n        if queued_jobs.is_empty() {\\n            return Ok(None);\\n        }\\n\\n        // Try to claim the first available job\\n        let now = now_ms();\\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\\n\\n        for job in queued_jobs {\\n            if self.task_store.claim_job(&job.id, &self.pod_id, claim_expires_at)? {\\n                // Successfully claimed\\n                debug!(\\n                    job_id = %job.id,\\n                    pod_id = %self.pod_id,\\n                    \\\"claimed Mode C job\\\"\\n                );\\n\\n                return Ok(Some(ClaimedJob {\\n                    id: job.id.clone(),\\n                    type_: job.type_.clone(),\\n                    params: job.params.clone(),\\n                    progress: job.progress.clone(),\\n                    claimed_by: self.pod_id.clone(),\\n                    claim_expires_at,\\n                    parent_job_id: job.parent_job_id.clone(),\\n                    chunk_index: job.chunk_index,\\n                    total_chunks: job.total_chunks,\\n                }));\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        // All queued jobs were claimed by another pod\\n        Ok(None)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Renew a job claim (heartbeat).\\n    ///\\n    /// Returns true if the claim was renewed, false if we lost the claim.\\n    pub fn renew_claim(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\\n        let now = now_ms();\\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\\n\\n        let renewed = self.task_store.renew_job_claim(job_id, claim_expires_at)?;\\n\\n        if !renewed {\\n            warn!(\\n                job_id = %job_id,\\n                pod_id = %self.pod_id,\\n                \\\"failed to renew job claim - may have lost ownership\\\"\\n            );\\n        }\\n\\n        Ok(renewed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Update job progress.\\n    pub fn update_progress(\\n        &self,\\n        job_id: &str,\\n        progress: &JobProgress,\\n        state: JobState,\\n    ) -> Result<()> {\\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(progress)\\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\\\"failed to serialize progress: {}\\\", e)))?;\\n\\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, state.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\\n\\n        debug!(\\n            job_id = %job_id,\\n            state = %state.as_str(),\\n            bytes_processed = progress.bytes_processed,\\n            \\\"updated job progress\\\"\\n        );\\n\\n        Ok(())\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Complete a job successfully.\\n    pub fn complete_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress) -> Result<()> {\\n        self.update_progress(job_id, progress, JobState::Completed)?;\\n\\n        info!(\\n            job_id = %job_id,\\n            \\\"completed Mode C job\\\"\\n        );\\n\\n        Ok(())\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Fail a job.\\n    pub fn fail_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress, error: String) -> Result<()> {\\n        let mut failed_progress = progress.clone();\\n        failed_progress.error = Some(error.clone());\\n\\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&failed_progress)\\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\\\"failed to serialize progress: {}\\\", e)))?;\\n\\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, JobState::Failed.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\\n\\n        error!(\\n            job_id = %job_id,\\n            error = %error,\\n            \\\"failed Mode C job\\\"\\n        );\\n\\n        Ok(())\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\\n    ///\\n    /// Called by the first pod that picks up a large job. The original job\\n    /// transitions to \\\"delegated\\\" state and child chunk jobs are created.\\n    pub fn split_job_into_chunks(\\n        &self,\\n        job: &ClaimedJob,\\n        chunk_specs: Vec<JobChunk>,\\n    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\\n        let params: JobParams = serde_json::from_str(&job.params)\\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\\\"failed to deserialize params: {}\\\", e)))?;\\n\\n        let total_chunks = chunk_specs.len() as u32;\\n        let mut chunk_job_ids = Vec::new();\\n\\n        // Mark the parent job as delegated (in_progress with special progress)\\n        let delegated_progress = JobProgress {\\n            bytes_processed: 0,\\n            docs_routed: 0,\\n            last_cursor: \\\"delegated\\\".to_string(),\\n            error: None,\\n        };\\n        self.update_progress(&job.id, &delegated_progress, JobState::InProgress)?;\\n\\n        // Create chunk jobs\\n        for (idx, chunk) in chunk_specs.iter().enumerate() {\\n            let mut chunk_params = params.clone();\\n            chunk_params.chunk = Some(chunk.clone());\\n\\n            let chunk_job_id = format!(\\\"{}-chunk-{}\\\", job.id, idx);\\n            let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&chunk_params)\\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\\\"failed to serialize chunk params: {}\\\", e)))?;\\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\\\"failed to serialize progress: {}\\\", e)))?;\\n\\n            let new_job = NewJob {\\n                id: chunk_job_id.clone(),\\n                type_: job.type_.clone(),\\n                params: params_json,\\n                state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\\n                progress: progress_json,\\n                parent_job_id: Some(job.id.clone()),\\n                chunk_index: Some(idx as i64),\\n                total_chunks: Some(total_chunks as i64),\\n            };\\n\\n            self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\\n            chunk_job_ids.push(chunk_job_id);\\n        }\\n\\n        info!(\\n            parent_job_id = %job.id,\\n            chunk_count = total_chunks,\\n            \\\"split job into chunks\\\"\\n        );\\n\\n        Ok(chunk_job_ids)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Reclaim expired claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Returns the number of claims reclaimed.\\n    pub fn reclaim_expired_claims(&self) -> Result<usize> {\\n        let now = now_ms();\\n        let expired_jobs = self.task_store.list_expired_claims(now)?;\\n\\n        let mut reclaimed = 0;\\n        for job in expired_jobs {\\n            // Reset the job to queued state\\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\\\"failed to serialize progress: {}\\\", e)))?;\\n\\n            // Clear claim and reset to queued\\n            // Note: We need to update the job to clear claimed_by and claim_expires_at\\n            // This is done via update_job_progress which also changes state to queued\\n            self.task_store.update_job_progress(&job.id, JobState::Queued.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\\n\\n            debug!(\\n                job_id = %job.id,\\n                previous_claimant = ?job.claimed_by,\\n                \\\"reclaimed expired job claim\\\"\\n            );\\n\\n            reclaimed += 1;\\n        }\\n\\n        if reclaimed > 0 {\\n            info!(\\n                count = reclaimed,\\n                \\\"reclaimed expired job claims\\\"\\n            );\\n        }\\n\\n        Ok(reclaimed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Get the queue depth (number of queued jobs).\\n    ///\\n    /// Used for HPA scaling per plan §14.4.\\n    pub fn queue_depth(&self) -> Result<u64> {\\n        self.task_store.count_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Get job by ID.\\n    pub fn get_job(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<JobRow>> {\\n        self.task_store.get_job(job_id)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// List all chunks for a parent job.\\n    pub fn list_chunks(&self, parent_job_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\\n        self.task_store.list_jobs_by_parent(parent_job_id)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// A claimed job being processed by a pod.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\npub struct ClaimedJob {\\n    /// Job ID.\\n    pub id: String,\\n    /// Job type.\\n    pub type_: String,\\n    /// Job parameters (JSON).\\n    pub params: String,\\n    /// Job progress (JSON).\\n    pub progress: String,\\n    /// Pod that claimed this job.\\n    pub claimed_by: String,\\n    /// When the claim expires (UNIX ms).\\n    pub claim_expires_at: i64,\\n    /// Parent job ID if this is a chunk.\\n    pub parent_job_id: Option<String>,\\n    /// Chunk index if this is a chunk.\\n    pub chunk_index: Option<i64>,\\n    /// Total chunks if this is part of a chunked job.\\n    pub total_chunks: Option<i64>,\\n}\\n\\nimpl ClaimedJob {\\n    /// Parse the job parameters.\\n    pub fn parse_params(&self) -> Result<JobParams> {\\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.params)\\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\\\"failed to deserialize params: {}\\\", e)))\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Parse the current progress.\\n    pub fn parse_progress(&self) -> Result<JobProgress> {\\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.progress)\\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\\\"failed to deserialize progress: {}\\\", e)))\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Check if this is a chunk job.\\n    pub fn is_chunk(&self) -> bool {\\n        self.parent_job_id.is_some()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Check if the claim is about to expire (within 5 seconds).\\n    pub fn claim_expiring_soon(&self) -> bool {\\n        let now = now_ms();\\n        self.claim_expires_at - now < 5_000\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_millis() as i64\\n}\\n\\n#[cfg(test)]\\nmod tests {\\n    use super::*;\\n    use crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore;\\n\\n    fn test_coordinator() -> ModeCCoordinator {\\n        let store = Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap());\\n        store.migrate().unwrap();\\n        ModeCCoordinator::new(store, \\\"test-pod\\\".to_string())\\n    }\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn test_job_state_roundtrip() {\\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\\\"queued\\\"), Some(JobState::Queued));\\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\\\"in_progress\\\"), Some(JobState::InProgress));\\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\\\"completed\\\"), Some(JobState::Completed));\\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\\\"failed\\\"), Some(JobState::Failed));\\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\\\"unknown\\\"), None);\\n\\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Queued.as_str(), \\\"queued\\\");\\n        assert_eq!(JobState::InProgress.as_str(), \\\"in_progress\\\");\\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Completed.as_str(), \\\"completed\\\");\\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Failed.as_str(), \\\"failed\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn test_job_type_roundtrip() {\\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\\\"dump_import\\\"), Some(JobType::DumpImport));\\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\\\"reshard_backfill\\\"), Some(JobType::ReshardBackfill));\\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\\\"unknown\\\"), None);\\n\\n        assert_eq!(JobType::DumpImport.as_str(), \\\"dump_import\\\");\\n        assert_eq!(JobType::ReshardBackfill.as_str(), \\\"reshard_backfill\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn test_enqueue_and_claim_job() {\\n        let coord = test_coordinator();\\n\\n        let params = JobParams {\\n            index_uid: \\\"test-index\\\".to_string(),\\n            primary_key: Some(\\\"id\\\".to_string()),\\n            shard_count: Some(64),\\n            old_shards: None,\\n            target_shards: None,\\n            shadow_index: None,\\n            chunk: None,\\n            source_url: Some(\\\"https://example.com/dump.ndjson\\\".to_string()),\\n            source_size_bytes: Some(1_000_000_000),\\n        };\\n\\n        let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\\n\\n        // Claim the job\\n        let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap();\\n        assert!(claimed.is_some());\\n        let claimed = claimed.unwrap();\\n        assert_eq!(claimed.id, job_id);\\n        assert_eq!(claimed.claimed_by, \\\"test-pod\\\");\\n\\n        // Parse params\\n        let parsed_params = claimed.parse_params().unwrap();\\n        assert_eq!(parsed_params.index_uid, \\\"test-index\\\");\\n        assert_eq!(parsed_params.primary_key, Some(\\\"id\\\".to_string()));\\n    }\\n\\n Mode C work-queued chunked jobs coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n//!\n//! Any pod can claim a queued job via compare-and-swap. Jobs have claim TTL\n//! with heartbeats; expired claims are released for reclamation.\n//!\n//! Large jobs are split into chunks on input boundaries by the first pod\n//! that picks them up. Each chunk is an independent job with a parent reference.\n//!\n//! Applied to:\n//! - §13.9 streaming dump import — chunks on NDJSON line boundaries\n//! - §13.1 reshard backfill — partitions by shard-id range\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::task_store::{JobRow, NewJob, TaskStore};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\n\n/// Job states (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum JobState {\n    /// Job is queued and waiting to be claimed.\n    Queued = 0,\n    /// Job is claimed and in progress.\n    InProgress = 1,\n    /// Job completed successfully.\n    Completed = 2,\n    /// Job failed.\n    Failed = 3,\n}\n\nimpl JobState {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"queued\" => Some(Self::Queued),\n            \"in_progress\" => Some(Self::InProgress),\n            \"completed\" => Some(Self::Completed),\n            \"failed\" => Some(Self::Failed),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Queued => \"queued\",\n            Self::InProgress => \"in_progress\",\n            Self::Completed => \"completed\",\n            Self::Failed => \"failed\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job types supported by Mode C coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum JobType {\n    /// Streaming dump import (plan §13.9).\n    DumpImport = 0,\n    /// Reshard backfill (plan §13.1).\n    ReshardBackfill = 1,\n}\n\nimpl JobType {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"dump_import\" => Some(Self::DumpImport),\n            \"reshard_backfill\" => Some(Self::ReshardBackfill),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::DumpImport => \"dump_import\",\n            Self::ReshardBackfill => \"reshard_backfill\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job progress tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobProgress {\n    /// Bytes processed so far (for dump import).\n    pub bytes_processed: u64,\n    /// Documents routed so far (for dump import).\n    pub docs_routed: u64,\n    /// Last cursor position for idempotent resume.\n    pub last_cursor: String,\n    /// Any error message.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for JobProgress {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: String::new(),\n            error: None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Chunk specification for a job.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobChunk {\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\n    pub index: u32,\n    /// Total number of chunks.\n    pub total: u32,\n    /// Starting position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub start: String,\n    /// Ending position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub end: String,\n    /// Estimated size in bytes.\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\n/// Job parameters.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobParams {\n    /// Index UID being operated on.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Primary key field (for dump import).\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Shard count (for dump import routing).\n    pub shard_count: Option<u32>,\n    /// Old shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub old_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Target shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub target_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Shadow index UID (for reshard backfill).\n    pub shadow_index: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk specification (if this is a chunk).\n    pub chunk: Option<JobChunk>,\n    /// Source data location (for dump import).\n    pub source_url: Option<String>,\n    /// Source data size (for dump import).\n    pub source_size_bytes: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Mode C job coordinator.\npub struct ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Task store for job persistence.\n    task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    /// Pod ID for claiming jobs.\n    pod_id: String,\n    /// Claim TTL in milliseconds (default 30s).\n    claim_ttl_ms: i64,\n    /// Heartbeat interval in milliseconds (default 10s).\n    heartbeat_interval_ms: i64,\n    /// Default chunk size in bytes (default 256 MiB).\n    default_chunk_size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\nimpl ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Create a new Mode C coordinator.\n    pub fn new(\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            task_store,\n            pod_id,\n            claim_ttl_ms: 30_000,  // 30 seconds\n            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10_000,  // 10 seconds\n            default_chunk_size_bytes: 268_435_456,  // 256 MiB\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the claim TTL.\n    pub fn with_claim_ttl_ms(mut self, ttl_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.claim_ttl_ms = ttl_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the heartbeat interval.\n    pub fn with_heartbeat_interval_ms(mut self, interval_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.heartbeat_interval_ms = interval_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the default chunk size.\n    pub fn with_chunk_size_bytes(mut self, size_bytes: u64) -> Self {\n        self.default_chunk_size_bytes = size_bytes;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Enqueue a new job.\n    pub fn enqueue_job(\n        &self,\n        type_: JobType,\n        params: JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<String> {\n        let job_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", type_.as_str(), uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n        let progress = JobProgress::default();\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let new_job = NewJob {\n            id: job_id.clone(),\n            type_: type_.as_str().to_string(),\n            params: params_json,\n            state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n            progress: progress_json,\n            parent_job_id: None,\n            chunk_index: None,\n            total_chunks: None,\n        };\n\n        self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            job_type = %type_.as_str(),\n            \"enqueued new Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Try to claim a queued job.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the claimed job if successful, or None if no jobs are available.\n    pub fn claim_job(&self) -> Result<Option<ClaimedJob>> {\n        // List queued jobs\n        let queued_jobs = self.task_store.list_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())?;\n\n        if queued_jobs.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        // Try to claim the first available job\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        for job in queued_jobs {\n            if self.task_store.claim_job(&job.id, &self.pod_id, claim_expires_at)? {\n                // Successfully claimed\n                debug!(\n                    job_id = %job.id,\n                    pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                    \"claimed Mode C job\"\n                );\n\n                return Ok(Some(ClaimedJob {\n                    id: job.id.clone(),\n                    type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                    params: job.params.clone(),\n                    progress: job.progress.clone(),\n                    claimed_by: self.pod_id.clone(),\n                    claim_expires_at,\n                    parent_job_id: job.parent_job_id.clone(),\n                    chunk_index: job.chunk_index,\n                    total_chunks: job.total_chunks,\n                }));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // All queued jobs were claimed by another pod\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Renew a job claim (heartbeat).\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if the claim was renewed, false if we lost the claim.\n    pub fn renew_claim(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        let renewed = self.task_store.renew_job_claim(job_id, claim_expires_at)?;\n\n        if !renewed {\n            warn!(\n                job_id = %job_id,\n                pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                \"failed to renew job claim - may have lost ownership\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(renewed)\n    }\n\n    /// Update job progress.\n    pub fn update_progress(\n        &self,\n        job_id: &str,\n        progress: &JobProgress,\n        state: JobState,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, state.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            state = %state.as_str(),\n            bytes_processed = progress.bytes_processed,\n            \"updated job progress\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete a job successfully.\n    pub fn complete_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress) -> Result<()> {\n        self.update_progress(job_id, progress, JobState::Completed)?;\n\n        info!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            \"completed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Fail a job.\n    pub fn fail_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress, error: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut failed_progress = progress.clone();\n        failed_progress.error = Some(error.clone());\n\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&failed_progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, JobState::Failed.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        error!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            error = %error,\n            \"failed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n    ///\n    /// Called by the first pod that picks up a large job. The original job\n    /// transitions to \"delegated\" state and child chunk jobs are created.\n    pub fn split_job_into_chunks(\n        &self,\n        job: &ClaimedJob,\n        chunk_specs: Vec<JobChunk>,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        let params: JobParams = serde_json::from_str(&job.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_chunks = chunk_specs.len() as u32;\n        let mut chunk_job_ids = Vec::new();\n\n        // Mark the parent job as delegated (in_progress with special progress)\n        let delegated_progress = JobProgress {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n            error: None,\n        };\n        self.update_progress(&job.id, &delegated_progress, JobState::InProgress)?;\n\n        // Create chunk jobs\n        for (idx, chunk) in chunk_specs.iter().enumerate() {\n            let mut chunk_params = params.clone();\n            chunk_params.chunk = Some(chunk.clone());\n\n            let chunk_job_id = format!(\"{}-chunk-{}\", job.id, idx);\n            let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&chunk_params)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize chunk params: {}\", e)))?;\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let new_job = NewJob {\n                id: chunk_job_id.clone(),\n                type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                params: params_json,\n                state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n                progress: progress_json,\n                parent_job_id: Some(job.id.clone()),\n                chunk_index: Some(idx as i64),\n                total_chunks: Some(total_chunks as i64),\n            };\n\n            self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n            chunk_job_ids.push(chunk_job_id);\n        }\n\n        info!(\n            parent_job_id = %job.id,\n            chunk_count = total_chunks,\n            \"split job into chunks\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(chunk_job_ids)\n    }\n\n    /// Reclaim expired claims.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the number of claims reclaimed.\n    pub fn reclaim_expired_claims(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let expired_jobs = self.task_store.list_expired_claims(now)?;\n\n        let mut reclaimed = 0;\n        for job in expired_jobs {\n            // Reset the job to queued state\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            // Clear claim and reset to queued\n            // Note: We need to update the job to clear claimed_by and claim_expires_at\n            // This is done via update_job_progress which also changes state to queued\n            self.task_store.update_job_progress(&job.id, JobState::Queued.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n            debug!(\n                job_id = %job.id,\n                previous_claimant = ?job.claimed_by,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claim\"\n            );\n\n            reclaimed += 1;\n        }\n\n        if reclaimed > 0 {\n            info!(\n                count = reclaimed,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claims\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(reclaimed)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the queue depth (number of queued jobs).\n    ///\n    /// Used for HPA scaling per plan §14.4.\n    pub fn queue_depth(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n        self.task_store.count_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())\n    }\n\n    /// Get job by ID.\n    pub fn get_job(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.get_job(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// List all chunks for a parent job.\n    pub fn list_chunks(&self, parent_job_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.list_jobs_by_parent(parent_job_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// A claimed job being processed by a pod.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimedJob {\n    /// Job ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Job type.\n    pub type_: String,\n    /// Job parameters (JSON).\n    pub params: String,\n    /// Job progress (JSON).\n    pub progress: String,\n    /// Pod that claimed this job.\n    pub claimed_by: String,\n    /// When the claim expires (UNIX ms).\n    pub claim_expires_at: i64,\n    /// Parent job ID if this is a chunk.\n    pub parent_job_id: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk index if this is a chunk.\n    pub chunk_index: Option<i64>,\n    /// Total chunks if this is part of a chunked job.\n    pub total_chunks: Option<i64>,\n}\n\nimpl ClaimedJob {\n    /// Parse the job parameters.\n    pub fn parse_params(&self) -> Result<JobParams> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Parse the current progress.\n    pub fn parse_progress(&self) -> Result<JobProgress> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize progress: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a chunk job.\n    pub fn is_chunk(&self) -> bool {\n        self.parent_job_id.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the claim is about to expire (within 5 seconds).\n    pub fn claim_expiring_soon(&self) -> bool {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        self.claim_expires_at - now < 5_000\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore;\n\n    fn test_coordinator() -> ModeCCoordinator {\n        let store = Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap());\n        store.migrate().unwrap();\n        ModeCCoordinator::new(store, \"test-pod\".to_string())\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_job_state_roundtrip() {\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"queued\"), Some(JobState::Queued));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"in_progress\"), Some(JobState::InProgress));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"completed\"), Some(JobState::Completed));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"failed\"), Some(JobState::Failed));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"unknown\"), None);\n\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Queued.as_str(), \"queued\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::InProgress.as_str(), \"in_progress\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Completed.as_str(), \"completed\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Failed.as_str(), \"failed\");\n    }\n\n Mode C work-queued chunked jobs coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n//!\n//! Any pod can claim a queued job via compare-and-swap. Jobs have claim TTL\n//! with heartbeats; expired claims are released for reclamation.\n//!\n//! Large jobs are split into chunks on input boundaries by the first pod\n//! that picks them up. Each chunk is an independent job with a parent reference.\n//!\n//! Applied to:\n//! - §13.9 streaming dump import — chunks on NDJSON line boundaries\n//! - §13.1 reshard backfill — partitions by shard-id range\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::task_store::{JobRow, NewJob, TaskStore};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\n\n/// Job states (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum JobState {\n    /// Job is queued and waiting to be claimed.\n    Queued = 0,\n    /// Job is claimed and in progress.\n    InProgress = 1,\n    /// Job completed successfully.\n    Completed = 2,\n    /// Job failed.\n    Failed = 3,\n}\n\nimpl JobState {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"queued\" => Some(Self::Queued),\n            \"in_progress\" => Some(Self::InProgress),\n            \"completed\" => Some(Self::Completed),\n            \"failed\" => Some(Self::Failed),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Queued => \"queued\",\n            Self::InProgress => \"in_progress\",\n            Self::Completed => \"completed\",\n            Self::Failed => \"failed\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job types supported by Mode C coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum JobType {\n    /// Streaming dump import (plan §13.9).\n    DumpImport = 0,\n    /// Reshard backfill (plan §13.1).\n    ReshardBackfill = 1,\n}\n\nimpl JobType {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"dump_import\" => Some(Self::DumpImport),\n            \"reshard_backfill\" => Some(Self::ReshardBackfill),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::DumpImport => \"dump_import\",\n            Self::ReshardBackfill => \"reshard_backfill\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job progress tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobProgress {\n    /// Bytes processed so far (for dump import).\n    pub bytes_processed: u64,\n    /// Documents routed so far (for dump import).\n    pub docs_routed: u64,\n    /// Last cursor position for idempotent resume.\n    pub last_cursor: String,\n    /// Any error message.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for JobProgress {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: String::new(),\n            error: None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Chunk specification for a job.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobChunk {\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\n    pub index: u32,\n    /// Total number of chunks.\n    pub total: u32,\n    /// Starting position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub start: String,\n    /// Ending position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub end: String,\n    /// Estimated size in bytes.\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\n/// Job parameters.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobParams {\n    /// Index UID being operated on.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Primary key field (for dump import).\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Shard count (for dump import routing).\n    pub shard_count: Option<u32>,\n    /// Old shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub old_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Target shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub target_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Shadow index UID (for reshard backfill).\n    pub shadow_index: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk specification (if this is a chunk).\n    pub chunk: Option<JobChunk>,\n    /// Source data location (for dump import).\n    pub source_url: Option<String>,\n    /// Source data size (for dump import).\n    pub source_size_bytes: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Mode C job coordinator.\npub struct ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Task store for job persistence.\n    task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    /// Pod ID for claiming jobs.\n    pod_id: String,\n    /// Claim TTL in milliseconds (default 30s).\n    claim_ttl_ms: i64,\n    /// Heartbeat interval in milliseconds (default 10s).\n    heartbeat_interval_ms: i64,\n    /// Default chunk size in bytes (default 256 MiB).\n    default_chunk_size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\nimpl ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Create a new Mode C coordinator.\n    pub fn new(\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            task_store,\n            pod_id,\n            claim_ttl_ms: 30_000,  // 30 seconds\n            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10_000,  // 10 seconds\n            default_chunk_size_bytes: 268_435_456,  // 256 MiB\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the claim TTL.\n    pub fn with_claim_ttl_ms(mut self, ttl_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.claim_ttl_ms = ttl_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the heartbeat interval.\n    pub fn with_heartbeat_interval_ms(mut self, interval_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.heartbeat_interval_ms = interval_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the default chunk size.\n    pub fn with_chunk_size_bytes(mut self, size_bytes: u64) -> Self {\n        self.default_chunk_size_bytes = size_bytes;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Enqueue a new job.\n    pub fn enqueue_job(\n        &self,\n        type_: JobType,\n        params: JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<String> {\n        let job_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", type_.as_str(), uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n        let progress = JobProgress::default();\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let new_job = NewJob {\n            id: job_id.clone(),\n            type_: type_.as_str().to_string(),\n            params: params_json,\n            state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n            progress: progress_json,\n            parent_job_id: None,\n            chunk_index: None,\n            total_chunks: None,\n        };\n\n        self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            job_type = %type_.as_str(),\n            \"enqueued new Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Try to claim a queued job.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the claimed job if successful, or None if no jobs are available.\n    pub fn claim_job(&self) -> Result<Option<ClaimedJob>> {\n        // List queued jobs\n        let queued_jobs = self.task_store.list_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())?;\n\n        if queued_jobs.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        // Try to claim the first available job\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        for job in queued_jobs {\n            if self.task_store.claim_job(&job.id, &self.pod_id, claim_expires_at)? {\n                // Successfully claimed\n                debug!(\n                    job_id = %job.id,\n                    pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                    \"claimed Mode C job\"\n                );\n\n                return Ok(Some(ClaimedJob {\n                    id: job.id.clone(),\n                    type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                    params: job.params.clone(),\n                    progress: job.progress.clone(),\n                    claimed_by: self.pod_id.clone(),\n                    claim_expires_at,\n                    parent_job_id: job.parent_job_id.clone(),\n                    chunk_index: job.chunk_index,\n                    total_chunks: job.total_chunks,\n                }));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // All queued jobs were claimed by another pod\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Renew a job claim (heartbeat).\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if the claim was renewed, false if we lost the claim.\n    pub fn renew_claim(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        let renewed = self.task_store.renew_job_claim(job_id, claim_expires_at)?;\n\n        if !renewed {\n            warn!(\n                job_id = %job_id,\n                pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                \"failed to renew job claim - may have lost ownership\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(renewed)\n    }\n\n    /// Update job progress.\n    pub fn update_progress(\n        &self,\n        job_id: &str,\n        progress: &JobProgress,\n        state: JobState,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, state.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            state = %state.as_str(),\n            bytes_processed = progress.bytes_processed,\n            \"updated job progress\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete a job successfully.\n    pub fn complete_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress) -> Result<()> {\n        self.update_progress(job_id, progress, JobState::Completed)?;\n\n        info!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            \"completed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Fail a job.\n    pub fn fail_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress, error: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut failed_progress = progress.clone();\n        failed_progress.error = Some(error.clone());\n\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&failed_progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, JobState::Failed.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        error!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            error = %error,\n            \"failed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n    ///\n    /// Called by the first pod that picks up a large job. The original job\n    /// transitions to \"delegated\" state and child chunk jobs are created.\n    pub fn split_job_into_chunks(\n        &self,\n        job: &ClaimedJob,\n        chunk_specs: Vec<JobChunk>,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        let params: JobParams = serde_json::from_str(&job.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_chunks = chunk_specs.len() as u32;\n        let mut chunk_job_ids = Vec::new();\n\n        // Mark the parent job as delegated (in_progress with special progress)\n        let delegated_progress = JobProgress {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n            error: None,\n        };\n        self.update_progress(&job.id, &delegated_progress, JobState::InProgress)?;\n\n        // Create chunk jobs\n        for (idx, chunk) in chunk_specs.iter().enumerate() {\n            let mut chunk_params = params.clone();\n            chunk_params.chunk = Some(chunk.clone());\n\n            let chunk_job_id = format!(\"{}-chunk-{}\", job.id, idx);\n            let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&chunk_params)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize chunk params: {}\", e)))?;\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let new_job = NewJob {\n                id: chunk_job_id.clone(),\n                type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                params: params_json,\n                state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n                progress: progress_json,\n                parent_job_id: Some(job.id.clone()),\n                chunk_index: Some(idx as i64),\n                total_chunks: Some(total_chunks as i64),\n            };\n\n            self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n            chunk_job_ids.push(chunk_job_id);\n        }\n\n        info!(\n            parent_job_id = %job.id,\n            chunk_count = total_chunks,\n            \"split job into chunks\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(chunk_job_ids)\n    }\n\n    /// Reclaim expired claims.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the number of claims reclaimed.\n    pub fn reclaim_expired_claims(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let expired_jobs = self.task_store.list_expired_claims(now)?;\n\n        let mut reclaimed = 0;\n        for job in expired_jobs {\n            // Reset the job to queued state\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            // Clear claim and reset to queued\n            // Note: We need to update the job to clear claimed_by and claim_expires_at\n            // This is done via update_job_progress which also changes state to queued\n            self.task_store.update_job_progress(&job.id, JobState::Queued.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n            debug!(\n                job_id = %job.id,\n                previous_claimant = ?job.claimed_by,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claim\"\n            );\n\n            reclaimed += 1;\n        }\n\n        if reclaimed > 0 {\n            info!(\n                count = reclaimed,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claims\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(reclaimed)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the queue depth (number of queued jobs).\n    ///\n    /// Used for HPA scaling per plan §14.4.\n    pub fn queue_depth(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n        self.task_store.count_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())\n    }\n\n    /// Get job by ID.\n    pub fn get_job(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.get_job(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// List all chunks for a parent job.\n    pub fn list_chunks(&self, parent_job_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.list_jobs_by_parent(parent_job_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// A claimed job being processed by a pod.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimedJob {\n    /// Job ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Job type.\n    pub type_: String,\n    /// Job parameters (JSON).\n    pub params: String,\n    /// Job progress (JSON).\n    pub progress: String,\n    /// Pod that claimed this job.\n    pub claimed_by: String,\n    /// When the claim expires (UNIX ms).\n    pub claim_expires_at: i64,\n    /// Parent job ID if this is a chunk.\n    pub parent_job_id: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk index if this is a chunk.\n    pub chunk_index: Option<i64>,\n    /// Total chunks if this is part of a chunked job.\n    pub total_chunks: Option<i64>,\n}\n\nimpl ClaimedJob {\n    /// Parse the job parameters.\n    pub fn parse_params(&self) -> Result<JobParams> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Parse the current progress.\n    pub fn parse_progress(&self) -> Result<JobProgress> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize progress: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a chunk job.\n    pub fn is_chunk(&self) -> bool {\n        self.parent_job_id.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the claim is about to expire (within 5 seconds).\n    pub fn claim_expiring_soon(&self) -> bool {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        self.claim_expires_at - now < 5_000\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore;\n\n    fn \"test-pod\".to_string())\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_job_state_roundtrip() {\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"queued\"), Some(JobState::Queued));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"in_progress\"), Some(JobState::InProgress));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"completed\"), Some(JobState::Completed));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"failed\"), Some(JobState::Failed));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"unknown\"), None);\n\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Queued.as_str(), \"queued\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::InProgress.as_str(), \"in_progress\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Completed.as_str(), \"completed\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Failed.as_str(), \"failed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_job_type_roundtrip() {\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\"dump_import\"), Some(JobType::DumpImport));\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\"reshard_backfill\"), Some(JobType::ReshardBackfill));\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\"unknown\"), None);\n\n        assert_eq!(JobType::DumpImport.as_str(), \"dump_import\");\n        assert_eq!(JobType::ReshardBackfill.as_str(), \"reshard_backfill\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_enqueue_and_claim_job() {\n        let coord = test_coordinator();\n\n        let params = target_shards: None,\n            shadow_index: None,\n            chunk: None,\n            source_url: Some(\"https://example.com/dump.ndjson\".to_string()),\n            source_size_bytes: Some(1_000_000_000),\n        };\n\n        let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim the job\n        let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap();\n        assert!(claimed.is_some());\n        let claimed = claimed.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claimed.id, job_id);\n        assert_eq!(claimed.claimed_by, \"test-pod\");\n\n        // Parse params\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_renewal() {\n        let coord = test_coordinator();\n\n        let params = JobParams {\n            index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n            primary_key: None,\n            shard_count: None,\n            old_shards: None,\n            target_shards: None,\n            shadow_index: None,\n            chunk: None,\n            source_url: None,\n            source_size_bytes: None,\n        };\n\n        let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::ReshardBackfill, params).unwrap();\n        let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap().unwrap();\n\n        // Renew the claim\n        let renewed = coord.renew_claim(&job_id).unwrap();\n        assert!(renewed);\n\n        // Get the job and verify claim was extended\n        let job = coord.get_job(&job_id).unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert!(job.claim_expires_at.unwrap() > claimed.claim_expires_at);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_split_job_into_chunks() {\n        let coord = \"0\".to_string(),\n                end: \"333333333\".to_string(),\n                size_bytes: 333_333_333,\n            },\n            JobChunk {\n                index: 1,\n                total: Each chunk can be processed independently by any pod.\\n\\nuse crate::mode_c_coordinator::{JobChunk, JobParams};\\nuse std::io::{BufRead, BufReader, Cursor};\\n\\n/// Chunk specification for a dump import.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\npub struct DumpChunkSpec {\\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\\n    pub index: u32,\\n    /// Total number of chunks.\\n    pub total: u32,\\n    /// Starting byte offset.\\n    pub start_offset: u64,\\n    /// Ending byte offset.\\n    pub end_offset: u64,\\n    /// Estimated size in bytes.\\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\\n}\\n\\n/// Split dump data into chunks on line boundaries.\\n///\\n/// Returns a vector of chunk specifications. bytes\\n///\\n/// # Returns\\n/// A vector of chunk specifications\\npub fn split_dump_into_chunks(data: &[u8], chunk_size_bytes: u64) -> Vec<DumpChunkSpec> {\\n    if data.is_empty() {\\n        return Vec::new();\\n    }\\n\\n    let total_size = data.len() as u64;\\n\\n    // If the data is smaller than the chunk size, return a single chunk\\n    if total_size <= chunk_size_bytes {\\n        return vec![DumpChunkSpec {\\n            index: 0,\\n            total: 1,\\n            start_offset: 0,\\n            end_offset: total_size,\\n            size_bytes: total_size,\\n        }];\\n Cursor::new(data);\\n    let reader = BufReader::new(cursor);\\n\\n    // Track line boundaries for chunking\\n    let mut line_start = 0u64;\\n    let mut last_line_end = 0u64;\\n\\n    for line_result in reader.lines() {\\n        match line_result {\\n            Ok(line) => {\\n                let line_bytes = line.len() as u64 + 1; // +1 for newline\\n                let line_end = last_line_end + line_bytes;\\n\\n                // Check if we've exceeded the chunk size since the last chunk start\\n                if line_end - current_offset >= chunk_size_bytes && current_offset < last_line_end {\\n                    // Create a chunk up to the previous line end\\n                    chunks.push(DumpChunkSpec {\\n                        index: chunk_index,\\n                        total: 0, // Will be filled in later\\n                        start_offset: current_offset,\\n                        end_offset: last_line_end,\\n                        size_bytes: last_line_end - current_offset,\\n                    });\\n\\n                    chunk_index += 1;\\n                    current_offset = last_line_end;\\n                }\\n\\n                last_line_end = line_end;\\n            }\\n            Err(_) => break,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Add the final chunk\\n    if current_offset < total_size {\\n         size_bytes: total_size - current_offset,\\n        });\\n    }\\n\\n    // Update the total count for all chunks\\n    let total = chunks.len() as u32;\\n    for chunk in &mut chunks {\\n        chunk.total = total;\\n    }\\n\\n    chunks\\n}\\n\\n/// Convert dump chunk specs to job chunks for the Mode C coordinator.\\npub fn dump_specs_to_job_chunks(specs: Vec<DumpChunkSpec>) -> Vec<JobChunk> {\\n    specs\\n        .into_iter()\\n        .map(|spec| JobChunk {\\n            index: spec.index,\\n            total: spec.total,\\n            start: spec.start_offset.to_string(),\\n            end: spec.end_offset.to_string(),\\n            size_bytes: spec.size_bytes,\\n        })\\n        .collect()\\n}\\n\\n/// Extract a chunk of data from create_test_data(100, 50);\\n        let chunk_size = 2_000; // Should get ~3-4 chunks\\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, chunk_size);\\n\\n        assert!(chunks.len() > 1);\\n\\n chunks\n        // Each line is about 70 bytes, so 100 lines = ~7KB\n        let data = create_test_data(100, 50);\n        let chunk_size = 2_000; // Should get ~3-4 chunks\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, chunk_size);\n\n        assert!(chunks.len() > 1);\n\n        // Verify chunks are sequential and cover the full range\n        let mut last_end = 0;\n        for (i, chunk) in chunks.iter().enumerate() {\n            assert_eq!(chunk.index, i as u32);\n            assert_eq!(chunk.start_offset, last_end);\n            assert!(chunk.end_offset > chunk.start_offset);\n            last_end = chunk.end_offset;\n        }\n\n        // Last chunk should end at the data size\n        assert_eq!(chunks.last().unwrap().end_offset, data.len() as Dump import chunking for Mode C coordinator (plan §13.9 + §14.5).\n//!\n//! Splits large NDJSON dumps into chunks on line boundaries.\n//! Each chunk can be processed independently by any pod.\n\nuse crate::mode_c_coordinator::{JobChunk, JobParams};\nuse std::io::{BufRead, BufReader, Cursor};\n\n/// Chunk specification for a dump import.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DumpChunkSpec {\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\n    pub index: u32,\n    /// Total number of chunks.\n    pub total: u32,\n    /// Starting byte offset.\n    pub start_offset: u64,\n    /// Ending byte offset.\n    pub end_offset: u64,\n    /// Estimated size in bytes.\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\n/// Split dump data into chunks on line boundaries.\n///\n/// Returns a vector of chunk specifications. Each chunk contains the\n/// byte offsets for processing that chunk of the dump.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `data` - The full dump data (NDJSON)\n/// * `chunk_size_bytes` - Target chunk size in bytes\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// A vector of chunk specifications\npub fn split_dump_into_chunks(data: &[u8], chunk_size_bytes: u64) -> Vec<DumpChunkSpec> {\n    if data.is_empty() {\n        return Vec::new();\n    }\n\n    let total_size = data.len() as u64;\n\n    // If the data is smaller than the chunk size, return a single chunk\n    if total_size <= chunk_size_bytes {\n        return vec![DumpChunkSpec {\n            index: 0,\n            total: 1,\n            start_offset: 0,\n            end_offset: total_size,\n            size_bytes: total_size,\n        }];\n    }\n\n    let mut chunks = Vec::new();\n    let mut current_offset: u64 = 0;\n    let mut chunk_index = 0u32;\n\n    // Use a cursor to read through the data\n    let cursor = Cursor::new(data);\n    let reader = BufReader::new(cursor);\n\n    // Track line boundaries for chunking\n    let mut line_start = 0u64;\n    let mut last_line_end = 0u64;\n\n    for line_result in reader.lines() {\n        match line_result {\n            Ok(line) => {\n                let line_bytes = line.len() as u64 + 1; // +1 for newline\n                let line_end = last_line_end + line_bytes;\n\n                // Check if we've exceeded the chunk size since the last chunk start\n                if line_end - current_offset >= chunk_size_bytes && current_offset < last_line_end {\n                    // Create a chunk up to the previous line end\n                    chunks.push(DumpChunkSpec {\n                        index: chunk_index,\n                        total: 0, // Will be filled in later\n                        start_offset: current_offset,\n                        end_offset: last_line_end,\n                        size_bytes: last_line_end - current_offset,\n                    });\n\n                    chunk_index += 1;\n                    current_offset = last_line_end;\n                }\n\n                last_line_end = line_end;\n            }\n            Err(_) => break,\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Add the final chunk\n    if current_offset < total_size {\n        chunks.push(DumpChunkSpec {\n            index: chunk_index,\n            total: 0, // Will be filled in later\n            start_offset: current_offset,\n            end_offset: total_size,\n            size_bytes: total_size - current_offset,\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Update the total count for all chunks\n    let total = chunks.len() as u32;\n    for chunk in &mut chunks {\n        chunk.total = total;\n    }\n\n    chunks\n}\n\n/// Convert dump chunk specs to job chunks for the Mode C coordinator.\npub fn dump_specs_to_job_chunks(specs: Vec<DumpChunkSpec>) -> Vec<JobChunk> {\n    specs\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|spec| JobChunk {\n            index: spec.index,\n            total: spec.total,\n            start: spec.start_offset.to_string(),\n            end: spec.end_offset.to_string(),\n            size_bytes: spec.size_bytes,\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Extract a chunk of data from the full dump.\n///\n/// Returns the byte slice for the specified chunk.\npub fn extract_chunk_data(data: &[u8], chunk: &DumpChunkSpec) -> &[u8] {\n    let start = chunk.start_offset as usize;\n    let end = chunk.end_offset as usize;\n    &data[start..end]\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn create_test_data(lines: usize, line_size: usize) -> Vec<u8> {\n        let mut data = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..lines {\n            let line = format!(\"{{\\\"id\\\": {}, \\\"data\\\": \\\"{}\\\"}}\\n\", i, \"x\".repeat(line_size));\n            data.extend_from_slice(line.as_bytes());\n        }\n        data\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_empty_data() {\n        let data = Vec::new();\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 1024);\n        assert!(chunks.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_small_data() {\n        let data = b\"{\\\"id\\\": 1}\\n{\\\"id\\\": 2}\\n\".to_vec();\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 1024);\n        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].start_offset, 0);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].end_offset, data.len() as u64);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_single_chunk() {\n        let data = create_test_data(10, 50);\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 10_000);\n        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].index, 0);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].total, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_multiple_chunks() {\n        // Create data that will split into multiple chunks\n        // Each line is about 70 bytes, so 100 lines = ~7KB\n        let data = create_test_data(100, 50);\n        let chunk_size = 2_000; // Should get ~3-4 chunks\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, chunk_size);\n\n        assert!(chunks.len() > 1);\n\n        // Verify chunks are sequential and cover the full range\n        let mut last_end = 0;\n        for (i, chunk) in chunks.iter().enumerate() {\n            assert_eq!(chunk.index, i as u32);\n            assert_eq!(chunk.start_offset, last_end);\n            assert!(chunk.end_offset > chunk.start_offset);\n            last_end = chunk.end_offset;\n        }\n\n        // Last chunk should end at the data size\n        assert_eq!(chunks.last().unwrap().end_offset, data.len() as u64);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_chunk_boundaries_on_lines() {\n        let data = create_test_data(20, 50);\n        let chunk_size = 500;\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, chunk_size);\n\n        // Verify each chunk starts and ends on line boundaries\n        for chunk in &chunks {\n            let chunk_data = extract_chunk_data(&data, chunk);\n\n            // Should start with valid JSON\n            assert!(chunk_data.starts_with(b\"{\"));\n            assert!(chunk_data.ends_with(b\"\\n\") || chunk_data.ends_with(b\"}\"));\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_chunk() {\n        let data = b\"line1\\nline2\\nline3\\n\".to_vec();\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 5);\n\n        for chunk in &chunks {\n            let chunk_data = extract_chunk_data(&data, chunk);\n            // Verify the chunk data is within bounds\n            assert!(chunk_data.len() <= (chunk.end_offset - chunk.start_offset) as usize);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_specs_to_job_chunks() {\n        let specs = vec![\n            DumpChunkSpec {\n                index: 0,\n                total: 2,\n                start_offset: 0,\n                end_offset: 100,\n                size_bytes: 100,\n            },\n            DumpChunkSpec {\n                index: 1,\n                total: 2,\n                start_offset: 100,\n                end_offset: 200,\n                size_bytes: 100,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        let job_chunks = dump_specs_to_job_chunks(specs);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].index, 0);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].total, 2);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].start, \"0\");\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].end, \"100\");\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[1].index, 1);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[1].start, \"100\");\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[1].end, \"200\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_large_file_chunking() {\n        // Simulate a 1GB file split into 256MB chunks\n        let line_size = 100;\n        let lines_per_chunk = (256 * 1024 * 1024) / line_size;\n        let total_lines = lines_per_chunk * 4; // 4 chunks\n\n        let data = create_test_data(total_lines as usize, line_size - 20);\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 256 * 1024 * 1024);\n\n        // Should get approximately 4 chunks\n        assert!(chunks.len() >= 3 && chunks.len() <= 5);\n\n        // Verify total coverage\n        let total_covered: u64 = chunks.iter().map(|c| c.size_bytes).sum();\n        assert_eq!(total_covered, data.len() as u64);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_chunks_cover_full_data() {\n        let data = create_test_data(1000, 100);\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 50_000);\n\n        let mut total_size = 0u64;\n        for chunk in &chunks {\n            total_size += chunk.size_bytes;\n        }\n\n        assert_eq!(total_size, data.len() as u64);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}
